questions.explanation: Faire takes avoir, and the subject is tu, so the auxiliary is auras plus the participle fait.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses le futur antérieur correctly?
options: Elle sera partie avant ton retour.
Elle aura partie avant ton retour.
Elle sera partir avant ton retour.
Elle avait partie avant ton retour.
questions.answer: Elle sera partie avant ton retour.
questions.explanation: Partir takes être, so the auxiliary is sera and the participle agrees with the feminine subject.

questions.prompt: Complete with the futur antérieur: 'Dès que nous ___ (recevoir) la réponse, nous vous préviendrons.'
questions.explanation: Recevoir takes avoir, and its past participle is reçu with a cedilla.

questions.prompt: Complete with the futur antérieur: 'Lundi prochain, elle ___ (rentrer) de vacances.'
questions.explanation: Rentrer takes être here, so the participle agrees with the feminine subject: rentrée.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I will lend you the book when I have read it.'
questions.explanation: The main clause stays in the simple future while the quand clause takes the futur antérieur.

activities.title: Organiser le déménagement
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is checking what will be ready before moving day, so answer with the futur antérieur.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu penses que tout sera prêt samedi ?
lines.en: Do you think everything will be ready on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'aurai emballé tous les cartons vendredi soir.
lines.en: Yes, I will have packed all the boxes by Friday evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la clé de l'appartement ?
lines.en: And the key to the flat?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dès que j'aurai signé le contrat, l'agence me la donnera.
lines.en: As soon as I have signed the contract, the agency will give it to me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je peux passer vers dix heures, alors ?
lines.en: So I can come round about ten?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Viens plutôt à midi : nous aurons déjà chargé la camionnette.
lines.en: Come at noon instead: we will already have loaded the van.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Parfait, je t'apporterai à manger.
lines.en: Perfect, I will bring you something to eat.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci, à midi nous aurons sûrement tout fini.
lines.en: Thanks, by noon we will surely have finished everything.

days.title: Les pronoms relatifs composés : lequel, auquel, duquel
days.objective: You can join two clauses with a relative pronoun that follows a preposition, choosing the right gender and number.
days.paretoFocus: Any sentence like 'the project I am working on' needs these forms, and they are everywhere in written and spoken French.
drills: Write five sentences describing objects in your home using 'avec lequel', 'dans laquelle' or 'sur lesquels'.
Turn three verbs that take à into relative clauses with auquel, à laquelle or auxquels.
Describe your street using 'à côté duquel' or 'en face de laquelle'.

items: What happens when à meets lequel, lesquels and lesquelles?
Why do we say 'à laquelle' in two words but 'auquel' in one?
Which form do you need after a compound preposition such as près de?

activities.title: Lequel et ses formes
activities.intro: After a preposition, French uses lequel, laquelle, lesquels or lesquelles, matching the noun they replace. The preposition à contracts into auquel, auxquels and auxquelles, but stays separate in à laquelle. After compound prepositions built on de, such as à côté de or près de, you need duquel, de laquelle, desquels or desquelles.

items.hr: C'est le projet auquel je pense depuis des mois.
items.en: This is the project I have been thinking about for months.
items.note: penser à plus a masculine noun gives auquel

items.hr: La réunion à laquelle j'ai assisté était très longue.
items.en: The meeting I attended was very long.
items.note: à laquelle stays in two words

items.hr: Voici les outils avec lesquels je travaille tous les jours.
items.en: Here are the tools I work with every day.

items.hr: L'entreprise pour laquelle il travaille est allemande.
items.en: The company he works for is German.

items.hr: Le bâtiment à côté duquel j'habite est en travaux.
items.en: The building I live next to is being renovated.
items.note: à côté de plus lequel gives duquel

activities.title: Choisir la bonne forme
activities.intro: Practise picking the relative pronoun that matches the preposition and the noun.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Le collègue ___ j'ai envoyé le dossier est en congé.'
questions.explanation: Envoyer quelque chose à quelqu'un needs à, and collègue here is masculine singular, so à plus lequel becomes auquel.

questions.prompt: Which form is correct after the preposition 'près de'?
options: le parc près duquel nous habitons
le parc près lequel nous habitons
le parc près dont nous habitons
le parc près auquel nous habitons
questions.answer: le parc près duquel nous habitons
questions.explanation: Près de ends in de, so lequel becomes duquel. Dont can never follow a compound preposition.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Ce sont des questions ___ je réfléchis souvent.'
questions.explanation: Réfléchir à plus a feminine plural noun gives auxquelles.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'C'est la société pour ___ elle travaille depuis dix ans.'
questions.explanation: Société is feminine singular, and pour does not contract, so the form is laquelle.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'This is the team with which I work every day.'
questions.explanation: Équipe is feminine singular, so avec is followed by laquelle.

activities.title: Présenter son service
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are showing a new colleague around, so describe people and things with lequel, auquel and duquel.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est quoi, ce dossier sur ton bureau ?
lines.en: What is that file on your desk?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est le projet auquel je consacre presque tout mon temps.
lines.en: It is the project I devote almost all my time to.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et avec qui tu travailles dessus ?
lines.en: And who do you work on it with?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Voici les deux collègues avec lesquels je le prépare.
lines.en: Here are the two colleagues I am preparing it with.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La salle de réunion est où, exactement ?
lines.en: Where exactly is the meeting room?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est la porte à côté de laquelle il y a la machine à café.
lines.en: It is the door next to which there is the coffee machine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et cette liste sur le mur ?
lines.en: And that list on the wall?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ce sont les règles auxquelles tout le monde doit faire attention.
lines.en: Those are the rules everyone has to pay attention to.

days.title: Les doubles pronoms : ordre et usage
days.objective: You can replace both the direct and the indirect object with pronouns and place them in the right order.
days.paretoFocus: Native speakers compress whole sentences into three words, and understanding 'je le lui ai dit' is essential for real conversation.
drills: Rewrite five sentences from your notes replacing both objects with pronouns.
Answer five yes-or-no questions about lending things, using two pronouns each time.
Say three affirmative commands with two pronouns, then make each one negative.

items: What is the order of pronouns before the verb in a normal statement?
How does the order change in an affirmative command?
Where do y and en go when another pronoun is present?

activities.title: Deux pronoms, un seul verbe
activities.intro: Before the verb, the order is me, te, nous, vous, then le, la, les, then lui, leur, then y, then en. In an affirmative command the pronouns come after the verb and the direct object comes first, joined by hyphens. In the negative command, the normal order comes back.

items.hr: Tu me le prêtes, s'il te plaît ?
items.en: Will you lend it to me, please?

items.hr: Je le lui ai déjà dit hier.
items.en: I already told him that yesterday.

items.hr: Ne nous en parle pas maintenant.
items.en: Do not talk to us about it now.

items.hr: Elle nous les a envoyés par courriel.
items.en: She sent them to us by email.
items.note: the participle agrees with les, so envoyés

items.hr: Rends-le-moi, s'il te plaît.
items.en: Give it back to me, please.
items.note: affirmative command: verb, direct object, then moi

activities.title: Mettre les pronoms dans l'ordre
activities.intro: Practise the order of two object pronouns.

questions.prompt: Replace both objects in 'Il donne les clés à Marie.'
questions.explanation: Les comes before lui, and Marie is one person, so the indirect pronoun is lui, not leur.

questions.prompt: Which affirmative command has the pronouns in the right order?
questions.explanation: In an affirmative command the direct object comes first, so le is followed by nous.

questions.prompt: Complete the answer to 'Tu as parlé du problème à tes parents ?' with two pronouns: 'Oui, je ___ ai parlé.'
questions.explanation: Parler à tes parents gives leur, parler du problème gives en, and en always comes last.

questions.prompt: Complete with one pronoun replacing 'la photo': 'Elle ne veut pas me ___ montrer.'
questions.explanation: Photo is feminine singular and is the direct object, so the pronoun is la, placed after me.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I will send them to you tonight.'
questions.explanation: Te belongs to the first group and therefore comes before the direct object les.

activities.title: Prêter du matériel
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour is borrowing and returning things, so answer using two pronouns each time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu peux me prêter ta perceuse ?
lines.en: Can you lend me your drill?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr, je te la prête jusqu'à dimanche.
lines.en: Of course, I will lend it to you until Sunday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et les vis, tu les as données à ton frère ?
lines.en: And the screws, did you give them to your brother?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je les lui ai données la semaine dernière.
lines.en: Yes, I gave them to him last week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as besoin des outils tout de suite ?
lines.en: Do you need the tools right away?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, rends-les-moi quand tu auras terminé.
lines.en: No, give them back to me when you have finished.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je dois en parler à ta femme aussi ?
lines.en: Do I have to talk to your wife about it too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ne lui en parle pas, elle déteste le bricolage.
lines.en: Do not talk to her about it, she hates DIY.

days.title: L'accord du participe passé : les cas qui posent problème
days.objective: You can decide when a past participle agrees and when it stays unchanged, including with reflexive verbs.
days.paretoFocus: These endings are silent in speech but visible in every written message, and getting them right is what separates a careful writer from a careless one.
drills: Write five sentences with que plus avoir, checking the ending of each participle.
Write three sentences with reflexive verbs where the body part follows the verb.
Find three past participles in your own notes and justify each ending in one line.

items: With avoir, when does the participle agree?
Why is there no agreement in 'elle s'est lavé les mains'?
Does the participle ever agree with en?

activities.title: Accorder ou ne pas accorder
activities.intro: With avoir, the participle agrees only with a direct object that comes before the verb. With reflexive verbs, agreement follows the same logic: it happens when the reflexive pronoun is the direct object, and not when another direct object follows. There is never agreement with en, and never with verbs whose reflexive pronoun is indirect.

items.hr: Les lettres que j'ai écrites sont sur la table.
items.en: The letters I wrote are on the table.
items.note: que is a direct object placed before the verb

items.hr: Elle s'est lavé les mains avant de manger.
items.en: She washed her hands before eating.
items.note: les mains follows, so no agreement

items.hr: Elles se sont parlé pendant deux heures.
items.en: They talked to each other for two hours.
items.note: parler à quelqu'un is indirect, so no agreement

items.hr: Des erreurs, j'en ai fait beaucoup.
items.en: Mistakes, I made a lot of them.

items.hr: La voiture qu'il a achetée est déjà en panne.
items.en: The car he bought has already broken down.
items.note: achetée agrees with the feminine voiture

activities.title: Vérifier les terminaisons
activities.intro: Practise deciding whether the past participle changes.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Les photos que tu m'as ___ sont magnifiques.'
questions.explanation: Que replaces les photos, a feminine plural direct object placed before the verb, so the participle takes es.

questions.prompt: Which sentence shows the correct agreement?
questions.explanation: Le doigt is the direct object and it comes after the verb, so the participle stays unchanged.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'prendre': 'Les décisions qu'ils ont ___ sont difficiles à accepter.'
questions.explanation: Que replaces les décisions, feminine plural and placed before the verb, so the participle is prises.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'se rencontrer': 'Elles se sont ___ à Lyon il y a trois ans.'
questions.explanation: Rencontrer quelqu'un is direct, so the reflexive pronoun is the direct object and the participle agrees.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The books I borrowed are already back on the shelf.'
questions.explanation: Que stands for les livres, a masculine plural direct object before the verb, so the participle ends in s.

activities.title: Retrouver les affaires perdues
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your flatmate is looking for missing things, so answer with relative clauses and correct participle agreement.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as vu les clés que j'ai posées ici ?
lines.en: Have you seen the keys I put down here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Les clés que tu as laissées sur la table sont dans le tiroir.
lines.en: The keys you left on the table are in the drawer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et les factures ? Je ne les trouve plus.
lines.en: And the bills? I cannot find them any more.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je les ai rangées dans le classeur bleu hier soir.
lines.en: I filed them in the blue folder last night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu t'es occupé du courrier aussi ?
lines.en: Did you deal with the post as well?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, et les lettres que j'ai ouvertes étaient toutes en anglais.
lines.en: Yes, and the letters I opened were all in English.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Merci, tu m'as vraiment sauvé la journée.
lines.en: Thanks, you really saved my day.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nous nous sommes bien organisés, finalement.
lines.en: We organised ourselves well, in the end.

days.title: Le discours indirect au passé : la concordance des temps
days.objective: You can report what someone said in the past, shifting tenses and time words correctly.
days.paretoFocus: Telling a story about a conversation is one of the most common things anyone does, and every tense has to move back one step.
drills: Report five things a friend told you yesterday, starting each with 'il a dit que'.
Turn three questions you were asked into indirect questions with si or ce que.
Rewrite three instructions you received using 'de' plus an infinitive.

items: What does the présent become when the reporting verb is in the past?
Which tense replaces the futur simple in reported speech?
How do you report a yes-or-no question?

activities.title: Rapporter des paroles
activities.intro: When the reporting verb is in the past, the tense of the quoted words moves back one step: présent becomes imparfait, passé composé becomes plus-que-parfait, and futur simple becomes conditionnel présent. Questions become clauses with si or ce que, and orders become de plus an infinitive. Time words also shift, so demain becomes le lendemain.

items.hr: Il a dit qu'il était très fatigué.
items.en: He said he was very tired.
items.note: je suis fatigué becomes il était fatigué

items.hr: Elle m'a expliqué qu'elle avait perdu son téléphone.
items.en: She explained that she had lost her phone.

items.hr: Ils ont annoncé qu'ils viendraient plus tard.
items.en: They announced that they would come later.

items.hr: Je lui ai demandé s'il connaissait la ville.
items.en: I asked him whether he knew the town.

items.hr: Le médecin m'a conseillé de me reposer une semaine.
items.en: The doctor advised me to rest for a week.
items.note: an order becomes de plus an infinitive

activities.title: Changer les temps
activities.intro: Practise shifting tenses when you report past words.

questions.prompt: 'Je pars demain', a-t-il dit. Il a dit qu'il ___ le lendemain.
questions.explanation: The quoted verb was in the présent, so after a past reporting verb it becomes the imparfait.

questions.prompt: 'J'ai tout compris', a-t-elle dit. Elle a dit qu'elle ___ tout compris.
questions.explanation: The passé composé shifts to the plus-que-parfait, so the auxiliary becomes avait.

questions.prompt: Report this promise: 'Nous téléphonerons ce soir.' Ils ont promis qu'ils ___ ce soir-là.
questions.explanation: The futur simple becomes the conditionnel présent, so téléphoneront becomes téléphoneraient.

questions.prompt: Report this question: 'Qu'est-ce que tu fais ?' Il m'a demandé ce que je ___.
questions.explanation: Qu'est-ce que becomes ce que, and the présent shifts to the imparfait.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'She told me she would come back the following day.'
questions.explanation: The future of the original words becomes the conditionnel, and demain becomes le lendemain.

activities.title: Raconter un entretien
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are telling a friend what happened at a job interview, so report everything that was said.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, qu'est-ce qu'ils t'ont dit ?
lines.en: So, what did they tell you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ils m'ont dit que le poste était encore libre.
lines.en: They told me the position was still open.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ils t'ont posé des questions difficiles ?
lines.en: Did they ask you difficult questions?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La directrice m'a demandé si j'avais déjà géré une équipe.
lines.en: The director asked me whether I had already managed a team.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour la réponse finale ?
lines.en: And what about the final answer?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Elle a promis qu'elle me rappellerait avant la fin de la semaine.
lines.en: She promised that she would call me back before the end of the week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ils t'ont donné des conseils ?
lines.en: Did they give you any advice?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, ils m'ont conseillé de préparer une présentation courte.
lines.en: Yes, they advised me to prepare a short presentation.

days.title: Exprimer la cause : parce que, car, puisque, grâce à, à cause de
days.objective: You can give reasons with five different connectors and choose the one that fits the situation.
days.paretoFocus: Explaining why is the backbone of everyday conversation, and French speakers notice immediately if you mix up grâce à and à cause de.
drills: Explain three recent decisions using parce que, then rewrite them with car.
Write two sentences with grâce à and two with à cause de, keeping the positive and negative values.
Use puisque three times to introduce a reason your listener already knows.

items: What is the difference in meaning between grâce à and à cause de?
Why can car never begin a sentence?
When would you prefer puisque to parce que?

activities.title: Dire pourquoi
activities.intro: Parce que gives new information and answers the question pourquoi. Car is slightly more formal and joins two clauses in the middle of a sentence. Puisque introduces a reason both speakers already accept. Grâce à and à cause de are followed by a noun, the first for a good result and the second for a bad one.

items.hr: Je suis rentré tôt parce que j'étais épuisé.
items.en: I came home early because I was exhausted.

items.hr: Il n'est pas venu, car il avait un rendez-vous.
items.en: He did not come, since he had an appointment.
items.note: car links clauses and never starts a sentence

items.hr: Puisque tu connais la ville, guide-nous.
items.en: Since you know the town, be our guide.
items.note: puisque introduces a shared, obvious reason

items.hr: Grâce à ses conseils, j'ai trouvé un travail.
items.en: Thanks to his advice, I found a job.
items.note: grâce à introduces a positive cause

items.hr: À cause de la grève, tous les trains sont annulés.
items.en: Because of the strike, all the trains are cancelled.
items.note: à cause de introduces a negative cause

activities.title: Choisir la bonne cause
activities.intro: Practise selecting the connector that matches the meaning.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ ta patience, tout s'est très bien passé.'
questions.explanation: The result is positive and a noun follows, so grâce à is the only possible choice.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces a reason the listener already knows?
questions.explanation: Puisque presents the cause as obvious or already shared, while parce que and car give new information.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word that introduces a positive cause: 'Il a réussi ___ à son travail acharné.'
questions.explanation: The full expression is grâce à, used when the cause leads to a favourable result.

questions.prompt: Complete with a conjunction of cause: 'Reste ici ___ il fait vraiment trop froid dehors.'
questions.explanation: A full clause follows, so a conjunction is needed rather than a preposition.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Since you already know the answer, you can help us.'
questions.explanation: Puisque can open the sentence because the reason is presented as already known.

activities.title: Expliquer un retard
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You arrive late to meet a friend, so explain the reasons using several different cause connectors.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Te voilà enfin ! Qu'est-ce qui s'est passé ?
lines.en: There you are at last! What happened?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis en retard à cause d'une panne de métro.
lines.en: I am late because of a breakdown on the underground.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu aurais pu m'envoyer un message.
lines.en: You could have sent me a message.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je n'ai pas pu, car mon téléphone était déchargé.
lines.en: I could not, since my phone was out of battery.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et tu as trouvé le chemin quand même ?
lines.en: And you found your way anyway?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, grâce à un passant très sympathique.
lines.en: Yes, thanks to a very friendly passer-by.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bon, on commande maintenant ?
lines.en: Right, shall we order now?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Puisque nous sommes en retard, commandons tout de suite.
lines.en: Since we are late, let us order straight away.

days.title: Exprimer la conséquence : donc, alors, si bien que, c'est pourquoi
days.objective: You can state the result of a situation with several connectors, including intensity structures like tellement que.
days.paretoFocus: Once you can chain a cause to its consequence, your speech stops sounding like a list of separate sentences.
drills: Write four cause-and-result pairs and join them with donc, alors, si bien que and c'est pourquoi.
Describe three situations using 'tellement ... que' or 'si ... que'.
Retell yesterday in five sentences where each result follows from the previous one.

items: Which mood follows si bien que?
What is the difference between si and tellement before an adjective?
Give a sentence where c'est pourquoi opens the second clause.

activities.title: Dire le résultat
activities.intro: Donc and alors are the everyday connectors of result, alors being the more spoken of the two. Si bien que and c'est pourquoi are slightly more formal and are always followed by the indicative. To add intensity, use si or tellement before an adjective or adverb, and tellement de or tant de before a noun, with que introducing the result.

items.hr: Il pleuvait sans arrêt, donc nous sommes restés à la maison.
items.en: It was raining non stop, so we stayed at home.
items.note: donc is neutral and very common

items.hr: J'ai raté mon train, alors j'ai pris le bus.
items.en: I missed my train, so I took the bus.
items.note: alors is typical of spoken French

items.hr: Il a beaucoup travaillé, si bien qu'il a réussi son examen.
items.en: He worked a lot, so much so that he passed his exam.
items.note: si bien que takes the indicative

items.hr: Le magasin ferme à six heures, c'est pourquoi je pars maintenant.
items.en: The shop closes at six, which is why I am leaving now.
items.note: c'est pourquoi is a little more formal

items.hr: Elle était tellement fatiguée qu'elle s'est endormie devant la télé.
items.en: She was so tired that she fell asleep in front of the television.

activities.title: Relier cause et résultat
activities.intro: Practise connectors that introduce a consequence.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'La route était bloquée, ___ nous avons fait demi-tour.'
questions.explanation: The second clause is the result of the first, and only alors expresses consequence here.

questions.prompt: Which mood follows the connector 'si bien que'?
questions.explanation: Consequence is presented as a real fact, so si bien que is always followed by the indicative.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word expressing quantity: 'Il y avait ___ de monde que nous n'avons pas pu entrer.'
questions.explanation: Before a noun introduced by de, French uses tellement de or tant de, with que introducing the result.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Le train a été supprimé, c'est ___ je suis arrivé en retard.'
questions.explanation: The fixed expression c'est pourquoi introduces the consequence of the previous statement.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'He spoke so fast that I understood nothing.'
questions.explanation: Si goes before the adverb vite, and que introduces the consequence.

activities.title: Une soirée annulée
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Explain to a friend the chain of events that ruined your evening, using connectors of consequence.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourquoi tu n'es pas venu hier soir ?
lines.en: Why did you not come yesterday evening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mon voisin a eu un problème, donc j'ai dû l'aider.
lines.en: My neighbour had a problem, so I had to help him.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ça a duré longtemps ?
lines.en: Did it take long?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il y avait tellement d'eau que nous avons appelé un plombier.
lines.en: There was so much water that we called a plumber.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et il est arrivé quand ?
lines.en: And when did he arrive?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vers minuit, si bien que je n'ai presque pas dormi.
lines.en: Around midnight, so much so that I hardly slept.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu dois être épuisé.
lines.en: You must be exhausted.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je le suis, c'est pourquoi je rentre tôt ce soir.
lines.en: I am, which is why I am going home early tonight.

days.title: Exprimer le but : pour que, afin de, de peur que
days.objective: You can say why you do something, choosing between an infinitive and a subjunctive clause according to the subject.
days.paretoFocus: Stating your purpose politely is essential at work and in requests, and the choice between pour and pour que is a classic French trap.
drills: Write three sentences with pour plus an infinitive about your own goals.
Rewrite the same three ideas with pour que and a different subject.
Write two sentences with de peur que about things you want to avoid.

items: Which structure do you use when both clauses share the same subject?
Which mood follows pour que and afin que?
How would you express a purpose that comes from fear?

activities.title: Dire dans quel but
activities.intro: When both actions have the same subject, French uses pour or afin de plus an infinitive. When the subjects are different, you need pour que, afin que or de peur que plus the subjunctive. Afin de and afin que are the more formal versions, and de peur que adds the idea of avoiding something unwanted.

items.hr: Je t'écris pour que tu sois au courant.
items.en: I am writing to you so that you are informed.
items.note: different subjects, so pour que plus subjunctive

items.hr: Il parle lentement afin d'être bien compris.
items.en: He speaks slowly in order to be clearly understood.
items.note: same subject, so afin de plus infinitive

items.hr: Nous partons tôt afin que les enfants puissent dormir.
items.en: We are leaving early so that the children can sleep.
items.note: pouvoir becomes puissent in the subjunctive

items.hr: Elle a tout vérifié de peur qu'il y ait une erreur.
items.en: She checked everything for fear that there might be a mistake.

items.hr: J'économise pour acheter une voiture d'occasion.
items.en: I am saving up to buy a second hand car.
items.note: same subject, so pour plus infinitive

activities.title: Infinitif ou subjonctif
activities.intro: Practise choosing the right structure of purpose.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses purpose correctly?
options: Je téléphone pour qu'il sache la nouvelle.
Je téléphone pour qu'il sait la nouvelle.
Je téléphone pour il sache la nouvelle.
Je téléphone afin il sache la nouvelle.
questions.answer: Je téléphone pour qu'il sache la nouvelle.
questions.explanation: The subjects are different, so pour que is required and savoir appears in the subjunctive form sache.

questions.prompt: When both clauses share the same subject, French uses:
questions.explanation: A subordinate clause is unnecessary when the subject does not change, so the infinitive is used.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'venir': 'Je te préviens à l'avance pour que tu ___ avec nous.'
questions.explanation: Pour que requires the subjunctive, and the tu form of venir is viennes.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'pouvoir': 'Range tes affaires afin que je ___ passer l'aspirateur.'
questions.explanation: Afin que takes the subjunctive, and the je form of pouvoir is puisse.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I am leaving early in order to avoid the traffic jams.'
questions.explanation: The subject is the same in both parts, so afin de plus the infinitive is correct.

activities.title: Préparer une présentation
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks why you are doing things a certain way, so answer with expressions of purpose.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourquoi tu envoies les documents aujourd'hui ?
lines.en: Why are you sending the documents today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pour que tout le monde ait le temps de les lire.
lines.en: So that everyone has time to read them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pourquoi tu répètes ta présentation à voix haute ?
lines.en: And why are you rehearsing your presentation out loud?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Afin de rester dans les dix minutes prévues.
lines.en: In order to stay within the ten minutes planned.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu imprimes aussi une copie papier ?
lines.en: Are you printing a paper copy too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, de peur que la connexion ne fonctionne pas.
lines.en: Yes, for fear that the connection does not work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu veux que je regarde tes diapositives ?
lines.en: Do you want me to look at your slides?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Volontiers, pour qu'un deuxième regard vérifie les chiffres.
lines.en: Gladly, so that a second pair of eyes checks the figures.

days.title: L'opposition et la concession : pourtant, bien que, malgré, alors que
days.objective: You can contrast two ideas and admit an obstacle, using both conjunctions and prepositions.
days.paretoFocus: Concession is what makes an opinion sound balanced and adult instead of flat, and it is expected in any discussion.
drills: Write three sentences with bien que about habits you keep despite a difficulty.
Rewrite each of them with malgré plus a noun.
Compare yourself with someone you know in three sentences using alors que.

items: Which mood follows bien que, and which one follows même si?
What kind of word must come after malgré?
Where does pourtant usually stand in the sentence?

activities.title: Malgré tout
activities.intro: Bien que and quoique are followed by the subjunctive, while même si always takes the indicative. Malgré is a preposition, so it must be followed by a noun and never by a clause. Pourtant is an adverb placed inside the second clause, and alors que simply contrasts two facts without any idea of obstacle.

items.hr: Bien qu'il soit malade, il continue à travailler.
items.en: Although he is ill, he keeps on working.

items.hr: Malgré la pluie, nous sommes sortis marcher.
items.en: In spite of the rain, we went out for a walk.
items.note: malgré is followed by a noun

items.hr: Il a beaucoup révisé, pourtant il a échoué.
items.en: He revised a lot, and yet he failed.
items.note: pourtant is an adverb, not a conjunction

items.hr: Elle aime le calme, alors que son frère adore la foule.
items.en: She likes quiet, whereas her brother loves crowds.

items.hr: Même s'il pleut demain, je viendrai.
items.en: Even if it rains tomorrow, I will come.
items.note: même si takes the indicative, never the subjunctive

activities.title: Nuancer une idée
activities.intro: Practise the connectors of contrast and concession.

questions.prompt: Which mood is required after 'bien que'?
questions.explanation: Bien que presents the obstacle as something not fully asserted, so it always takes the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ ses efforts, il n'a pas gagné la course.'
questions.explanation: A noun group follows, so only the preposition malgré works here.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'avoir': 'Bien qu'elle ___ peu de temps, elle nous aide toujours.'
questions.explanation: Bien que requires the subjunctive, and the elle form of avoir is ait.

questions.prompt: Complete with one adverb of contrast: 'C'est un film très long ; il n'est ___ jamais ennuyeux.'
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed inside the clause, since no conjunction can appear in that position.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Although it is expensive, I am going to buy it.'
questions.explanation: Bien que opens the concessive clause and être appears in the subjunctive form soit.

activities.title: Vivre en ville ou à la campagne
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Defend your choice of where to live, admitting the drawbacks with concessive structures.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu ne trouves pas la ville trop bruyante ?
lines.en: Do you not find the city too noisy?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien que ce soit bruyant, j'aime avoir tout à côté.
lines.en: Although it is noisy, I like having everything nearby.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Les loyers sont énormes, quand même.
lines.en: The rents are huge, all the same.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Malgré le prix, je gagne deux heures de transport par jour.
lines.en: In spite of the price, I save two hours of commuting a day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Moi, je préfère le calme du village.
lines.en: Personally, I prefer the quiet of the village.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu as une belle vue, alors que moi je vois un parking.
lines.en: You have a lovely view, whereas I look at a car park.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu déménagerais un jour ?
lines.en: Would you move one day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Même si je gagnais plus, je resterais ici.
lines.en: Even if I earned more, I would stay here.

days.title: L'hypothèse irréelle du passé : si + plus-que-parfait et conditionnel passé
days.objective: You can talk about what would have happened if the past had been different, using si plus the plus-que-parfait.
days.paretoFocus: Regrets, excuses and second guessing fill everyday conversation, and this is the only structure French uses for them.
drills: Write three regrets about last week beginning with 'si j'avais'.
Turn three real past events into their opposite and state the imagined result.
Write two sentences where the condition is past but the result is still present.

items: Which tense follows si in an unreal past sentence?
How do you build the conditionnel passé?
Can the conditionnel ever appear directly after si?

activities.title: Si j'avais su
activities.intro: To imagine a different past, French pairs a si clause in the plus-que-parfait with a main clause in the conditionnel passé, which is the conditional of avoir or être plus a past participle. The conditionnel never appears directly after si. Sometimes the condition is past but the result is present, and then the main clause uses the conditionnel présent.

items.hr: Si j'avais su, je ne serais pas venu.
items.en: If I had known, I would not have come.

items.hr: Si tu m'avais prévenu, j'aurais pu t'aider.
items.en: If you had warned me, I could have helped you.

items.hr: Nous serions arrivés à l'heure si nous étions partis plus tôt.
items.en: We would have arrived on time if we had left earlier.
items.note: the si clause can come second

items.hr: Ils auraient réussi s'ils avaient travaillé davantage.
items.en: They would have succeeded if they had worked harder.

items.hr: Si elle n'avait pas raté son train, elle serait déjà là.
items.en: If she had not missed her train, she would already be here.

activities.title: Imaginer un autre passé
activities.intro: Practise the two tenses of the unreal past.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Si j'avais eu ton numéro, je t'___ tout de suite.'
questions.explanation: The si clause is in the plus-que-parfait, so the result clause takes the conditionnel passé.

questions.prompt: After 'si' in an unreal past sentence, French uses:
questions.explanation: Si is never followed by a conditional form, and the unreal past condition is carried by the plus-que-parfait.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'prendre': 'Si nous ___ le train, nous aurions évité les embouteillages.'
questions.explanation: The condition needs the plus-que-parfait, formed with avions plus the participle pris.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'venir': 'Elle ___ si tu l'avais invitée à temps.'
questions.explanation: Venir takes être, so the conditionnel passé is serait plus the participle, which agrees with the feminine subject.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'If you had told me, I would have waited for you.'
questions.explanation: The si clause carries the plus-que-parfait and the main clause carries the conditionnel passé.

activities.title: Après le match
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend is going over a disappointing match, so react with unreal past hypotheses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quel dommage, on a perdu de deux points.
lines.en: What a shame, we lost by two points.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si le gardien n'était pas tombé, on aurait gagné.
lines.en: If the goalkeeper had not fallen, we would have won.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: L'entraîneur a changé la tactique trop tard.
lines.en: The coach changed the tactics too late.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: S'il avait réagi à la mi-temps, tout aurait été différent.
lines.en: If he had reacted at half time, everything would have been different.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et toi, tu regrettes de ne pas être venu au stade ?
lines.en: And do you regret not coming to the stadium?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si j'avais acheté un billet, je serais encore là-bas.
lines.en: If I had bought a ticket, I would still be over there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La prochaine fois, on y va ensemble.
lines.en: Next time we will go together.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord, si tu m'avais proposé plus tôt, j'aurais dit oui.
lines.en: All right, if you had suggested it earlier, I would have said yes.

days.title: Le subjonctif après les verbes d'opinion à la forme négative
days.objective: You can use the subjunctive after verbs of opinion when they are negative or in a question.
days.paretoFocus: Disagreeing politely is a daily need, and 'je ne pense pas que' immediately marks whether your French is careful or approximate.
drills: Write four opinions you disagree with, each starting 'je ne crois pas que'.
Turn three statements into questions with 'penses-tu que' plus a subjunctive.
Say the same idea twice, once affirmatively with the indicative and once negatively with the subjunctive.

items: Which mood follows 'je pense que' and which one follows 'je ne pense pas que'?
Give the subjunctive forms of être and avoir in the il form.
Why does a question sometimes allow the subjunctive?

activities.title: Je ne crois pas que ce soit vrai
activities.intro: Penser, croire, trouver and être sûr take the indicative when they are affirmative, because the speaker asserts a fact. When they are negative, the fact is no longer asserted and the subjunctive appears. In questions, both moods are possible, and the subjunctive adds a shade of doubt.

items.hr: Je ne pense pas qu'il vienne ce soir.
items.en: I do not think he is coming tonight.
items.note: affirmative it would be je pense qu'il vient

items.hr: Elle ne croit pas que ce soit une bonne idée.
items.en: She does not believe that it is a good idea.

items.hr: Je ne suis pas sûr qu'il ait raison.
items.en: I am not sure he is right.

items.hr: Penses-tu qu'elle puisse nous aider ?
items.en: Do you think she can help us?
items.note: the question form also allows the subjunctive

items.hr: Il ne trouve pas que le film vaille le détour.
items.en: He does not think the film is worth the trip.

activities.title: Douter poliment
activities.intro: Decide whether each sentence below calls for the indicative or the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Je ne crois pas qu'il ___ prêt pour l'examen.'
questions.explanation: The verb of opinion is negative, so the subordinate clause takes the subjunctive form soit.

questions.prompt: Which sentence keeps the indicative correctly after an affirmative opinion?
questions.explanation: An affirmative verb of opinion asserts the fact, so the indicative a is required.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'faire': 'Je ne suis pas certain qu'il ___ beau demain.'
questions.explanation: Être certain in the negative triggers the subjunctive, and the il form of faire is fasse.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'savoir': 'Elle ne pense pas que nous ___ toute la vérité.'
questions.explanation: The nous form of savoir in the subjunctive is sachions.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I do not think that this solution is possible.'
questions.explanation: The negative opinion verb forces the subjunctive soit instead of est.

activities.title: Discuter d'un projet risqué
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is enthusiastic about a plan you doubt, so express your reservations with negative opinion verbs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je vais ouvrir un restaurant l'année prochaine.
lines.en: I am going to open a restaurant next year.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne suis pas sûr que ce soit le bon moment.
lines.en: I am not sure it is the right time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourquoi ? Le quartier est très vivant.
lines.en: Why? The area is very lively.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne pense pas qu'il y ait assez de clients en hiver.
lines.en: I do not think there are enough customers in winter.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La banque m'a promis un prêt.
lines.en: The bank has promised me a loan.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Crois-tu vraiment qu'elle puisse tout financer ?
lines.en: Do you really believe it can finance everything?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu es pessimiste, comme toujours.
lines.en: You are pessimistic, as always.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne trouve pas que ce soit du pessimisme, juste de la prudence.
lines.en: I do not think it is pessimism, just caution.

days.title: La voix passive : former et choisir entre actif et passif
days.objective: You can build passive sentences in several tenses and know when French prefers an active alternative.
days.paretoFocus: News, notices and formal writing are full of passives, and knowing when to switch back to on keeps your own French natural.
drills: Turn five active sentences from your notes into the passive.
Write three passive sentences without naming the agent.
Rewrite three of those passives with on plus an active verb.

items: What does the past participle agree with in a passive sentence?
Which preposition introduces the agent?
When would a French speaker use on instead of the passive?

activities.title: Être plus participe passé
activities.intro: The passive is formed with être in the required tense plus the past participle, which agrees with the subject. The agent, when mentioned, is introduced by par. French often leaves the agent out, and in everyday speech it very often replaces the passive with on plus an active verb.

items.hr: Le rapport a été rédigé par une stagiaire.
items.en: The report was written by an intern.

items.hr: Cette maison est construite en pierre.
items.en: This house is built of stone.

items.hr: Les résultats seront publiés lundi matin.
items.en: The results will be published on Monday morning.

items.hr: Le problème a été résolu très rapidement.
items.en: The problem was solved very quickly.
items.note: the agent can simply be left out

items.hr: On a volé mon vélo devant la gare.
items.en: My bike was stolen outside the station.
items.note: French often prefers on to a passive here

activities.title: Actif ou passif
activities.intro: Practise transforming sentences into the passive and back.

questions.prompt: Which is the passive form of 'Le directeur signe le contrat.'?
options: Le contrat est signé par le directeur.
Le contrat a signé par le directeur.
Le contrat est signant par le directeur.
questions.answer: Le contrat est signé par le directeur.
questions.explanation: The object becomes the subject, être appears in the same tense as the active verb, and par introduces the agent.

questions.prompt: In a passive sentence, the past participle agrees with:
questions.explanation: With être the participle always takes the gender and number of the subject.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'construire': 'Ces immeubles ont été ___ dans les années soixante.'
questions.explanation: The subject is masculine plural, so the participle takes an s.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition: 'La lettre a été signée ___ le maire lui-même.'
questions.explanation: The agent of a passive action is introduced by par.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The results will be announced tomorrow.'
questions.explanation: The future of être is seront, and the participle agrees with the masculine plural subject.

activities.title: Les travaux du quartier
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Report neighbourhood news to a neighbour, using passive sentences where the agent is unknown or official.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu sais pourquoi la rue est fermée ?
lines.en: Do you know why the street is closed?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Elle a été fermée par la mairie pour des travaux.
lines.en: It was closed by the town hall for roadworks.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ça va durer longtemps ?
lines.en: Is it going to last long?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Les trottoirs seront refaits avant la fin du mois.
lines.en: The pavements will be redone before the end of the month.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et l'ancienne école, elle devient quoi ?
lines.en: And the old school, what is happening to it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Elle a été transformée en bibliothèque l'an dernier.
lines.en: It was turned into a library last year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Personne ne nous prévient jamais.
lines.en: Nobody ever tells us anything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: On a distribué des lettres, mais beaucoup ne les ont pas reçues.
lines.en: Letters were handed out, but many people did not get them.

days.title: La nominalisation : transformer un verbe en nom
days.objective: You can turn a verb into the matching noun and use it to write shorter, denser sentences.
days.paretoFocus: Headlines, notices and formal writing are built almost entirely on nouns, so this doubles your reading comfort.
drills: List ten verbs you use often and write the matching noun with its article.
Rewrite three of your own sentences replacing the verb with its noun.
Write three short headline style phrases with no conjugated verb at all.

items: Which nouns come from partir, ouvrir and construire?
Which suffix is the most common for nouns made from verbs?
Give one noun that is formed without any suffix at all.

activities.title: Du verbe au nom
activities.intro: Many French nouns are built from verbs with a suffix such as tion, ment, age, ure or ée. Others use no suffix at all and simply reuse the stem. Learning the noun with its article is essential, because the gender is not predictable from the verb.

items.hr: L'augmentation des prix inquiète les habitants.
items.en: The rise in prices worries the residents.

items.hr: Le départ du train est prévu à huit heures.
items.en: The departure of the train is scheduled for eight o'clock.
items.note: partir gives le départ, with no suffix

items.hr: L'ouverture du nouveau magasin a eu lieu samedi.
items.en: The opening of the new shop took place on Saturday.

items.hr: La construction de l'école durera deux ans.
items.en: The construction of the school will take two years.

items.hr: La baisse du chômage reste très lente.
items.en: The fall in unemployment remains very slow.
items.note: baisser gives une baisse, again with no suffix

activities.title: Trouver le nom
activities.intro: Practise forming and using nouns derived from verbs.

questions.prompt: Which noun corresponds to the verb 'développer'?
questions.explanation: Verbs of this family form nouns in ment, and the noun is masculine.

questions.prompt: Which noun corresponds to the verb 'diminuer'?
questions.explanation: The suffix tion is the most productive one, and nouns ending in tion are always feminine.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun built from 'arriver', including its article: 'Nous attendons ___ du train de Lille.'
questions.explanation: Arriver gives une arrivée, and before a vowel the article is elided to l'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun built from 'fermer': 'La ___ du magasin a surpris tous les habitants.'
questions.explanation: Fermer gives la fermeture, with the feminine suffix ure.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The opening of the museum is planned for June.'
questions.explanation: The noun ouverture is feminine, so the adjective prévue takes an e.

activities.title: Commenter les nouvelles
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Summarise pieces of news for a friend, replacing verbs with the matching nouns.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce qu'ils disent dans le journal ce matin ?
lines.en: What are they saying in the paper this morning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ils annoncent une augmentation du prix de l'électricité.
lines.en: They are announcing a rise in the price of electricity.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Encore ? Et pour les transports ?
lines.en: Again? And what about transport?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La construction de la nouvelle ligne commencera en septembre.
lines.en: The construction of the new line will begin in September.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y a aussi quelque chose sur l'emploi ?
lines.en: Is there anything about employment too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, une légère baisse du chômage dans la région.
lines.en: Yes, a slight fall in unemployment in the region.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la bibliothèque du centre ?
lines.en: And the library in the centre?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sa fermeture est prévue pendant tout l'été.
lines.en: Its closure is planned for the whole summer.

days.title: Les indéfinis avancés : n'importe quel, certains, la plupart
days.objective: You can talk about unspecified people, things and quantities with the main indefinite words and agree the verb correctly.
days.paretoFocus: These words let you generalise without exaggerating, which is exactly what any discussion of society or habits requires.
drills: Write three sentences about your friends using la plupart, certains and quelques-uns.
Use n'importe quel, n'importe qui and n'importe quoi in three natural sentences.
Describe a group you belong to using chacun in two sentences.

items: Is the verb singular or plural after la plupart des?
How does n'importe quel change before a feminine noun?
Which indefinite word is always followed by a singular verb?

activities.title: N'importe qui, la plupart, chacun
activities.intro: N'importe quel behaves like an adjective and agrees with its noun, while n'importe qui and n'importe quoi stand alone. Certains and quelques-uns refer to part of a group and take a plural verb. La plupart des is always followed by a plural verb, even though the word itself looks singular, and chacun is always singular.

items.hr: Tu peux passer n'importe quel jour de la semaine.
items.en: You can come round any day of the week.

items.hr: N'importe qui peut s'inscrire à ce cours.
items.en: Anyone at all can sign up for this class.

items.hr: Certains collègues préfèrent le télétravail.
items.en: Some colleagues prefer working from home.
items.note: certains is followed by a plural noun

items.hr: La plupart des étudiants travaillent le week-end.
items.en: Most students work at the weekend.
items.note: la plupart des takes a plural verb

items.hr: Chacun apporte quelque chose à manger.
items.en: Everyone brings something to eat.

activities.title: Généraliser sans exagérer
activities.intro: Practise indefinite words and the agreement they require.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'La plupart des gens ___ d'accord avec cette idée.'
questions.explanation: La plupart des is followed by a plural verb because the real subject is the plural noun.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Appelle-moi à ___ heure, ça ne me dérange pas.'
questions.explanation: Heure is feminine singular, so quel becomes quelle.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Quelques-uns sont partis tôt, mais ___ sont restés jusqu'à la fin.'
questions.explanation: Certains refers to another part of the same group and takes a plural verb.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Il ne dit pas n'importe ___ : il vérifie toujours ses sources.'
questions.explanation: N'importe quoi refers to things and stands alone, without a following noun.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Most of my friends live abroad.'
questions.explanation: Before a possessive, la plupart is followed by de alone, and the verb stays plural.

activities.title: Organiser un repas de groupe
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are planning a shared meal, so talk about the group with indefinite words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On fait ça quel jour, finalement ?
lines.en: Which day are we doing this, in the end?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: N'importe quel samedi me va, je suis libre.
lines.en: Any Saturday suits me, I am free.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tout le monde mange de la viande ?
lines.en: Does everyone eat meat?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, certains sont végétariens depuis des années.
lines.en: No, some of them have been vegetarian for years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il faut préparer beaucoup de plats, alors.
lines.en: We will have to prepare a lot of dishes, then.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Chacun apporte un plat, ce sera plus simple.
lines.en: Everyone brings a dish, it will be simpler.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour la boisson ?
lines.en: And what about drinks?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La plupart des invités boivent surtout de l'eau.
lines.en: Most of the guests mainly drink water.

days.title: Les expressions de temps : dès que, tant que, au fur et à mesure
days.objective: You can link two actions in time with precise conjunctions and choose the right tense or mood after each one.
days.paretoFocus: Time linking is what turns separate facts into a story or a plan, and these conjunctions appear constantly in spoken French.
drills: Write three plans with dès que plus a future or futur antérieur.
Write two conditions with tant que about things that will stay true.
Describe a gradual change in two sentences with au fur et à mesure que.

items: Which of these time conjunctions is followed by the subjunctive?
Which tense usually follows dès que when you talk about the future?
What is the difference between tant que and jusqu'à ce que?

activities.title: Dès que, tant que, jusqu'à ce que
activities.intro: Dès que and une fois que mean that one action starts immediately after another, and in future contexts they take the futur simple or the futur antérieur, not the present. Tant que means for as long as a situation lasts. Au fur et à mesure que describes two things progressing together. Only jusqu'à ce que requires the subjunctive.

items.hr: Dès que j'aurai fini, je te préviendrai.
items.en: As soon as I have finished, I will let you know.
items.note: dès que with the futur antérieur

items.hr: Tant que tu es là, je ne m'inquiète pas.
items.en: As long as you are here, I do not worry.
items.note: tant que covers the whole duration

items.hr: Au fur et à mesure que le temps passe, ça devient plus facile.
items.en: As time goes by, it becomes easier.

items.hr: Une fois que tu auras compris la règle, tout ira mieux.
items.en: Once you have understood the rule, everything will be better.
items.note: une fois que works like dès que

items.hr: Attends ici jusqu'à ce que je revienne.
items.en: Wait here until I come back.
items.note: jusqu'à ce que takes the subjunctive

activities.title: Situer deux actions
activities.intro: Practise time conjunctions and the forms that follow them.

questions.prompt: Which time expression is followed by the subjunctive?
questions.explanation: The others present the action as a real fact and take the indicative, while jusqu'à ce que looks at a limit not yet reached.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ que tu ne signeras pas, rien ne sera officiel.'
questions.explanation: Tant que covers a whole period during which the situation remains unchanged.

questions.prompt: Complete with the two words meaning 'as soon as': 'Je t'appelle ___ ___ j'arrive à la gare.'
questions.explanation: The expression is made of a preposition and the conjunction que, and it introduces an immediate sequence.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct form of 'partir': 'Reste avec nous jusqu'à ce que le dernier invité ___.'
questions.explanation: Jusqu'à ce que requires the subjunctive, and the il form of partir is parte.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'As soon as the film has finished, we will go home.'
questions.explanation: French uses a future form after dès que, where English uses a present perfect.

activities.title: Attendre les résultats
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are waiting for exam results, so link your plans in time with these conjunctions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu sauras quand, exactement ?
lines.en: When exactly will you know?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dès que la liste sera publiée, je regarderai en ligne.
lines.en: As soon as the list has been published, I will look online.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu vas travailler en attendant ?
lines.en: Are you going to work in the meantime?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tant que je n'ai pas les résultats, je continue à réviser.
lines.en: As long as I do not have the results, I keep revising.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ce n'est pas trop stressant ?
lines.en: Is that not too stressful?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Au fur et à mesure que les jours passent, je suis plus calme.
lines.en: As the days go by, I am calmer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On fête ça quand ?
lines.en: When are we celebrating?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Attends jusqu'à ce que je sois sûr d'avoir réussi.
lines.en: Wait until I am sure I have passed.

days.title: Réviser les temps du passé : choisir entre trois temps
days.objective: You can tell a story in the past, switching correctly between imparfait, passé composé and plus-que-parfait.
days.paretoFocus: Narrating what happened is the single most common thing anyone does in a foreign language, and the wrong tense changes the meaning.
drills: Tell the story of your last trip in six sentences using all three past tenses.
Write three sentences where the plus-que-parfait explains a later situation.
Describe an interrupted action three times with different verbs.

items: Which tense sets the scene and which one moves the story forward?
When do you need the plus-que-parfait?
Which tense do you use for a repeated childhood habit?

activities.title: Raconter au passé
activities.intro: The imparfait describes the background, ongoing situations, repeated habits and states. The passé composé reports the events that move the story forward. The plus-que-parfait places an action further back, before another past action. In a single sentence, the interrupted action is in the imparfait and the interrupting event is in the passé composé.

items.hr: Hier soir, je lisais quand le téléphone a sonné.
items.en: Yesterday evening I was reading when the phone rang.
items.note: background in the imparfait, event in the passé composé

items.hr: Quand je suis arrivé, ils avaient déjà mangé.
items.en: When I arrived, they had already eaten.
items.note: the plus-que-parfait comes first in time

items.hr: Chaque été, nous allions chez nos grands-parents.
items.en: Every summer we used to go to our grandparents' house.

items.hr: Il a plu toute la journée de samedi.
items.en: It rained all day on Saturday.
items.note: passé composé for a finished, framed action

items.hr: Elle était fatiguée parce qu'elle avait mal dormi.
items.en: She was tired because she had slept badly.
items.note: a past state explained by an earlier action

activities.title: Choisir le bon temps
activities.intro: Practise selecting between the three past tenses.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Nous ___ la télévision quand l'orage a éclaté.'
questions.explanation: The action was in progress and got interrupted, so it takes the imparfait.

questions.prompt: Which tense shows an action completed before another past action?
questions.explanation: The plus-que-parfait is built with the imparfait of the auxiliary and places the action further back in time.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right past form of 'oublier': 'Je ne pouvais pas ouvrir la porte parce que j'___ mes clés.'
questions.explanation: Forgetting the keys happened before the moment described, so the plus-que-parfait is needed.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right past form of 'décider': 'Ce jour-là, elle ___ de changer complètement de travail.'
questions.explanation: This is a single completed event that moves the story forward, so the passé composé is used.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'We were eating when they arrived.'
questions.explanation: The ongoing action takes the imparfait and the sudden event takes the passé composé.

activities.title: Raconter une mésaventure
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Tell a friend about a small disaster, mixing the three past tenses naturally.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as l'air fatigué, ta journée s'est mal passée ?
lines.en: You look tired, did your day go badly?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je dormais encore quand mon voisin a sonné à la porte.
lines.en: I was still asleep when my neighbour rang the doorbell.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce qu'il voulait ?
lines.en: What did he want?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il avait laissé ses clés à l'intérieur et il attendait dehors.
lines.en: He had left his keys inside and he was waiting outside.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as pu faire quelque chose ?
lines.en: Were you able to do anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai appelé un serrurier, mais il est arrivé deux heures plus tard.
lines.en: I called a locksmith, but he arrived two hours later.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ton travail dans tout ça ?
lines.en: And what about your work in all this?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quand je suis enfin parti, la réunion avait déjà commencé.
lines.en: When I finally left, the meeting had already started.

days.title: La santé et le bien-être : parler de ses habitudes
days.objective: You can describe your daily health habits and their effects using il m'arrive de, avoir tendance à and depuis que.
days.paretoFocus: These three frames replace vague adverbs and let you talk about frequency, tendencies and changes in almost any everyday conversation.
drills: Write five sentences about your own week with il m'arrive de, each using a different verb.
List three habits you would like to change and say what you tend to do instead, using avoir tendance à.
Say aloud what has improved in your life since a change you made, starting with Depuis que.

items: How do you say 'I tend to work too much' in French?
Which preposition follows aider quelqu'un before an infinitive?
Give the French frame that means 'it sometimes happens to me that I...'.

activities.title: Parler de ses habitudes de santé
activities.intro: French rarely relies on adverbs alone to talk about habits. Everyday speech uses set frames: il m'arrive de + infinitive (sometimes I), avoir tendance à + infinitive (to tend to) and aider quelqu'un à + infinitive. Depuis que introduces the change that caused a new state.

items.hr: Il m'arrive de sauter le petit-déjeuner quand je suis pressé.
items.en: Sometimes I skip breakfast when I'm in a hurry.
items.note: il m'arrive de + infinitif = it happens to me now and then

items.hr: J'ai tendance à me coucher trop tard le week-end.
items.en: I tend to go to bed too late at the weekend.
items.note: avoir tendance à, no article before tendance

items.hr: Marcher une demi-heure par jour m'aide à mieux dormir.
items.en: Walking half an hour a day helps me sleep better.

items.hr: J'essaie de faire du sport deux fois par semaine, mais je n'y arrive pas toujours.
items.en: I try to do sport twice a week, but I don't always manage it.

items.hr: Depuis que j'ai arrêté le café le soir, je me sens beaucoup mieux.
items.en: Since I stopped drinking coffee in the evening, I feel much better.

activities.title: Habitudes et bien-être
activities.intro: Practise talking about frequency, tendencies and the effects of a change.

questions.prompt: Choose the natural way to say 'I tend to snack in the afternoon.'
options: J'ai la tendance de grignoter l'après-midi.
Je suis tendance à grignoter l'après-midi.
questions.explanation: The expression is fixed: avoir + tendance with no article + à + infinitive.

questions.prompt: Which sentence says that something happens to you occasionally?
options: Il m'arrive d'oublier de boire de l'eau.
J'arrive d'oublier de boire de l'eau.
Il arrive moi d'oublier de boire de l'eau.
Il m'arrive oublier de boire de l'eau.
questions.answer: Il m'arrive d'oublier de boire de l'eau.
questions.explanation: The structure is impersonal: il arrive à quelqu'un de + infinitive, so the pronoun is indirect (m') and de is compulsory.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition: 'Le yoga m'aide ___ me détendre après le travail.'
questions.explanation: aider quelqu'un takes à before an infinitive, never de.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Depuis ___ je fais du vélo, j'ai beaucoup plus d'énergie.'
questions.explanation: Before a full clause with its own subject and verb you need depuis que; depuis alone only introduces a time expression.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Sometimes I forget to take my medicine.'
questions.explanation: Il m'arrive de + infinitive expresses an occasional habit, and oublier keeps its own de before the second infinitive.

activities.title: Chez le médecin
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A doctor quizzes you on your routine; admit an occasional lapse, a bad tendency, and when it all started.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, qu'est-ce qui vous amène aujourd'hui ?
lines.en: So, what brings you here today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis très fatigué en ce moment, et il m'arrive de m'endormir devant l'ordinateur.
lines.en: I'm very tired at the moment, and sometimes I fall asleep in front of the computer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous dormez combien d'heures par nuit ?
lines.en: How many hours a night do you sleep?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Six, au maximum. J'ai tendance à me coucher après minuit.
lines.en: Six at most. I tend to go to bed after midnight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et vous bougez un peu dans la journée ?
lines.en: And do you move around a bit during the day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Depuis que je travaille chez moi, presque plus. Marcher le matin m'aiderait sans doute à mieux dormir.
lines.en: Since I've been working from home, hardly at all. Walking in the morning would probably help me sleep better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est exactement ce que j'allais vous conseiller.
lines.en: That's exactly what I was going to suggest.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alors je vais essayer de m'y tenir.
lines.en: Then I'll try to stick to it.

days.title: Le logement et la vie urbaine : avantages et inconvénients
days.objective: You can weigh up two places to live, naming advantages and drawbacks with l'avantage, c'est que and contrasting with alors que and en revanche.
days.paretoFocus: The advantage and drawback frames are the backbone of any balanced opinion, at work, in an exam or in conversation.
drills: Write four sentences about your own town, two starting with L'avantage and two with L'inconvénient.
Contrast your current home with a previous one in three sentences using alors que.
Say aloud what bothers you most about your neighbourhood, starting with Ce qui me dérange le plus.

items: Which mood follows l'inconvénient, c'est que?
Give two French connectors that introduce a contrast.
How do you highlight what you like most about a place in one sentence?

activities.title: Peser le pour et le contre
activities.intro: French uses fixed frames to weigh up options: l'avantage de + infinitive, c'est que + indicative, and l'inconvénient, c'est que. The little c'est que before the clause is what makes it sound native. En revanche and alors que then mark the contrast.

items.hr: L'avantage de vivre en centre-ville, c'est qu'on peut tout faire à pied.
items.en: The advantage of living in the town centre is that you can do everything on foot.
items.note: L'avantage de + infinitif, c'est que + indicatif

items.hr: L'inconvénient, c'est que les loyers sont beaucoup trop chers.
items.en: The drawback is that rents are far too expensive.

items.hr: En banlieue, les logements sont plus grands ; en revanche, il faut une voiture.
items.en: In the suburbs homes are bigger; on the other hand, you need a car.
items.note: en revanche = on the other hand, slightly formal

items.hr: Mon studio est bien situé alors que celui de ma sœur est loin de tout.
items.en: My studio flat is well located whereas my sister's is far from everything.

items.hr: Ce qui me dérange le plus, c'est le bruit de la rue.
items.en: What bothers me most is the noise from the street.
items.note: Ce qui + verbe, c'est ... highlights the subject

activities.title: Vivre en ville ou en périphérie
activities.intro: Practise presenting advantages, drawbacks and contrasts.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct way to introduce a drawback.
options: L'inconvénient, c'est qu'il n'y a pas de parking.
L'inconvénient, c'est qu'il n'y ait pas de parking.
L'inconvénient de qu'il n'y a pas de parking.
L'inconvénient, c'est n'y a pas de parking.
questions.answer: L'inconvénient, c'est qu'il n'y a pas de parking.
questions.explanation: After l'inconvénient, c'est que the verb stays in the indicative; this frame states a fact, so no subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces a contrast in a fairly formal register?
questions.explanation: En revanche marks a contrast; en effet confirms, en plus adds, c'est-à-dire reformulates.

questions.prompt: Complete the contrast: 'Mon quartier est très calme ___ que le centre est bruyant.'
questions.explanation: alors que sets two simultaneous facts against each other.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun that means drawback: 'L'___, c'est que les loyers augmentent chaque année.'
questions.explanation: un inconvénient is the opposite of un avantage and takes the frame c'est que + indicative.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The advantage of the suburbs is that the flats are bigger.'
questions.explanation: L'avantage de + noun is picked up by c'est que before the clause; French keeps that resumptive c'est.

activities.title: Chercher un appartement
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Compare two flats with a friend using l'avantage, l'inconvénient and alors que.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, tu as visité les deux appartements ?
lines.en: So, did you view both flats?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui. L'avantage du premier, c'est qu'il est à dix minutes du travail.
lines.en: Yes. The advantage of the first one is that it's ten minutes from work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et l'inconvénient ?
lines.en: And the drawback?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le loyer est énorme, et il n'y a aucun rangement.
lines.en: The rent is huge, and there's no storage at all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le deuxième, il est comment ?
lines.en: And what's the second one like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Beaucoup plus grand ; en revanche, il faut prendre le train tous les matins.
lines.en: Much bigger; on the other hand, you have to take the train every morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce qui compte le plus pour toi ?
lines.en: What matters most to you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ce qui compte le plus, c'est le temps de trajet, alors que je pensais d'abord au prix.
lines.en: What matters most is the travel time, whereas at first I was thinking about the price.

days.title: Les transports et la mobilité : se déplacer autrement
days.objective: You can describe how you travel and how long a journey takes, using en and à with vehicles, mettre + duration + pour and il faut compter.
days.paretoFocus: Journey time and means of transport come up every time you arrange a meeting, a trip or a commute.
drills: Describe three journeys you make regularly, each with mettre + duration + pour.
List five means of transport and write whether each takes en or à.
Compare the cost of two ways of reaching a nearby city in two sentences.

items: Do you say en vélo or à vélo?
Which verb expresses the time a trip takes?
What does il faut compter deux heures mean?

activities.title: Se déplacer autrement
activities.intro: Use en for vehicles you sit inside (en train, en voiture) and à for ones you are on or use uncovered (à pied, à vélo). For duration, mettre + time + pour + infinitive says how long it takes you, while the impersonal il faut compter + time says how much time to allow.

items.hr: Je mets vingt minutes pour aller au travail à vélo.
items.en: It takes me twenty minutes to get to work by bike.

items.hr: En bus, il faut compter presque une heure aux heures de pointe.
items.en: By bus you have to allow almost an hour at rush hour.

items.hr: Depuis un an, je covoiture avec un collègue pour partager les frais.
items.en: For the past year I've been carsharing with a colleague to share the costs.

items.hr: La trottinette, c'est pratique, mais je ne me sens pas en sécurité.
items.en: Scooters are handy, but I don't feel safe on them.

items.hr: Le train revient moins cher que la voiture si on réserve à l'avance.
items.en: The train works out cheaper than the car if you book in advance.
items.note: revenir cher = to work out expensive

activities.title: Trajets et horaires
activities.intro: Practise prepositions of transport and ways of stating journey time.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'Je vais au bureau ___ métro.'
questions.explanation: en is used with vehicles you travel inside: en métro, en voiture, en train.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'It takes me forty minutes to get there'?
options: Je mets quarante minutes pour y aller.
Je prends quarante minutes pour y aller.
Ça me met quarante minutes d'y aller.
Il me faut quarante minutes d'aller.
questions.answer: Je mets quarante minutes pour y aller.
questions.explanation: mettre + duration + pour + infinitive is the standard structure when the traveller is the subject.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Aux heures de pointe, il faut ___ une bonne heure de trajet.'
questions.explanation: il faut compter + duration means you should allow that much time.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition: 'Le week-end, je fais mes courses ___ pied.'
questions.explanation: à is used when you are not enclosed in a vehicle: à pied, à vélo, à moto.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The train works out cheaper if you book in advance.'
questions.explanation: revenir cher expresses the final cost, and si + present introduces a real condition with a present result.

activities.title: Organiser un trajet
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Work out with a colleague how much time to allow for tomorrow's trip.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment tu comptes venir au séminaire lundi ?
lines.en: How are you planning to get to the seminar on Monday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En train, je pense. Je mets deux heures pour y aller, porte à porte.
lines.en: By train, I think. It takes me two hours to get there, door to door.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: En voiture, ce serait plus rapide, non ?
lines.en: By car it would be quicker, wouldn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas sûr. Avec les bouchons, il faut compter au moins trois heures.
lines.en: Not necessarily. With the traffic jams you have to allow at least three hours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On pourrait covoiturer et partager les frais.
lines.en: We could carshare and split the costs.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Volontiers, mais le train revient moins cher si je réserve maintenant.
lines.en: Gladly, but the train works out cheaper if I book now.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comme tu veux. On se retrouve sur place, alors.
lines.en: As you like. We'll meet there, then.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, j'arrive vers neuf heures moins le quart.
lines.en: Perfect, I'll get there around a quarter to nine.

days.title: La gastronomie : parler de cuisine et de goûts
days.objective: You can comment on a dish and explain a simple recipe using avoir un goût de, un peu trop + adjective and cooking verbs like faire revenir.
days.paretoFocus: Being able to praise, criticise politely and follow a recipe covers almost every food conversation you will have.
drills: Say what three dishes you ate this week tasted like, naming the flavour each one reminded you of.
Criticise two dishes politely with un peu trop + adjective.
