Write the first three steps of a recipe you know using cooking verbs in the imperative.

items: How do you say 'it has a slight lemon taste' in French?
Which structure softens the criticism 'it's too salty'?
What does faire revenir mean in a recipe?

activities.title: Parler de cuisine et de goûts
activities.intro: To describe a flavour, French uses avoir un goût de + noun. To criticise politely, place un peu before trop + adjective. Recipes use the imperative with cooking verbs such as faire revenir (to fry lightly) and laisser mijoter (to let simmer).

items.hr: Ce plat a un petit goût de citron, c'est très rafraîchissant.
items.en: This dish has a slight lemon taste, it's very refreshing.

items.hr: Pour moi, c'est un peu trop salé.
items.en: For me it's a bit too salty.
items.note: un peu trop + adjectif softens the criticism

items.hr: Faites revenir les oignons dans un peu d'huile, puis laissez mijoter vingt minutes.
items.en: Fry the onions lightly in a little oil, then let it simmer for twenty minutes.

items.hr: Je ne suis pas très viande, je prendrai plutôt le plat végétarien.
items.en: I'm not big on meat, I'll have the vegetarian dish instead.
items.note: ne pas être très + nom, informal way to say you are not a fan

items.hr: Je raffole des desserts au chocolat.
items.en: I'm crazy about chocolate desserts.

activities.title: Au restaurant et en cuisine
activities.intro: Practise describing flavours, softening criticism and reading recipe steps.

questions.prompt: Which sentence politely says a dish is too salty for you?
options: Je trouve ça un peu trop salé.
Je trouve ça très salé trop.
questions.answer: Je trouve ça un peu trop salé.
questions.explanation: un peu trop + adjective softens a criticism, and trop always comes before the adjective.

questions.prompt: Complete naturally: 'Cette sauce ___ un goût de noisette.'
questions.explanation: avoir un goût de is the fixed expression used to describe a flavour.

questions.prompt: Complete the recipe step, meaning to fry them lightly at the start: 'Faites ___ les oignons dans un peu d'huile.'
questions.explanation: faire revenir means to fry ingredients lightly before the main cooking.

questions.prompt: Complete, meaning that you are crazy about them: 'Je ___ des fruits de mer, j'en mange dès que je peux.'
questions.explanation: raffoler de + noun expresses a strong liking and is always followed by de.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Then let it simmer for twenty minutes over a low heat.'
questions.explanation: laisser + infinitive gives the recipe instruction, and heat level is expressed with à feu doux or à feu vif.

activities.title: Un dîner entre amis
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Compliment the cook on a meal, guess its flavours, then get the instructions to make it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, tu en penses quoi de mon curry ?
lines.en: So, what do you think of my curry?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il est excellent. Il a un léger goût de coco, non ?
lines.en: It's excellent. It has a slight coconut taste, doesn't it?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, j'ai ajouté du lait de coco à la fin.
lines.en: Yes, I added coconut milk at the end.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pour moi, il est juste un peu trop épicé, mais c'est vraiment bon.
lines.en: For me it's just a bit too spicy, but it's really good.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu veux la recette ?
lines.en: Do you want the recipe?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Volontiers. On fait revenir les oignons d'abord, c'est ça ?
lines.en: Gladly. You fry the onions lightly first, is that right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exactement, puis on laisse mijoter une demi-heure.
lines.en: Exactly, then you let it simmer for half an hour.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, je raffole de ce genre de plats.
lines.en: Perfect, I'm crazy about that kind of dish.

days.title: L'éducation et la formation : parcours et diplômes
days.objective: You can narrate your educational path using faire des études de, s'inscrire en, passer versus réussir, and une fois que.
days.paretoFocus: Explaining what you studied is asked in almost every introduction, and the passer versus réussir trap causes real misunderstandings.
drills: Write your own study path in five sentences in chronological order.
Write two sentences with passer un examen and two with réussir un examen, keeping the meanings distinct.
Describe what you did once you finished your studies, using Une fois que.

items: Does j'ai passé mon examen mean you took it or you succeeded?
Which preposition follows s'inscrire when naming a course?
How do you say 'I am currently taking a training course'?

activities.title: Raconter son parcours d'études
activities.intro: Describing a study path combines the passé composé for the steps with fixed phrases: s'inscrire en, faire des études de, suivre une formation. Beware of the false friend: passer un examen means to sit it, while réussir means to pass it.

items.hr: J'ai fait des études de droit pendant trois ans.
items.en: I studied law for three years.
items.note: faire des études de + matière, no article

items.hr: Je me suis inscrit en licence d'histoire après le lycée.
items.en: I enrolled on a history degree after secondary school.

items.hr: J'ai passé le concours deux fois avant de le réussir.
items.en: I sat the competitive exam twice before passing it.
items.note: passer = to sit, réussir = to pass

items.hr: Une fois que j'ai eu mon diplôme, j'ai cherché un stage.
items.en: Once I had my degree, I looked for an internship.

items.hr: Je suis en train de suivre une formation en comptabilité.
items.en: I'm currently doing a training course in accounting.
items.note: suivre une formation = to take a course

activities.title: Parcours et diplômes
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of studies and the order of events in a career path.

questions.prompt: A friend says 'J'ai passé mon examen.' What does that tell you?
options: He is preparing for the exam.
questions.explanation: passer un examen means to take it; to say you passed you need réussir un examen or obtenir son diplôme.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct phrase: '___ des études de médecine à Lyon.'
questions.explanation: faire des études de + subject is the standard way of saying what someone studied.

questions.prompt: Complete, meaning that you enrolled: 'Je me suis ___ en master de sociologie l'année dernière.'
questions.explanation: s'inscrire en + course; after être the past participle agrees with the subject.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Une ___ que j'ai eu mon diplôme, j'ai commencé à travailler.'
questions.explanation: une fois que + a completed past tense means 'once something had happened'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I am currently taking a training course in computing.'
questions.explanation: être en train de + infinitive marks an action in progress, and formations are named with en + field.

activities.title: Expliquer son parcours
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Walk a new colleague through your studies step by step, from enrolment to your diploma.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as étudié quoi, exactement ?
lines.en: What exactly did you study?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai fait des études de gestion, puis je me suis inscrit en master de marketing.
lines.en: I studied management, then I enrolled on a marketing master's.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu travaillais pendant tes études ?
lines.en: Did you work during your studies?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un peu. J'ai aussi passé un concours à la fin, que j'ai réussi de justesse.
lines.en: A bit. I also sat a competitive exam at the end, which I only just passed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et après le diplôme ?
lines.en: And after the degree?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Une fois que j'ai eu mon diplôme, j'ai fait un stage de six mois ici.
lines.en: Once I had my degree, I did a six-month internship here.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu continues à te former ?
lines.en: Are you still doing training?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je suis en train de suivre une formation en analyse de données.
lines.en: Yes, I'm currently doing a training course in data analysis.

days.title: Chercher un emploi : la lettre de motivation
days.objective: You can write the key sentences of a French cover letter, from Suite à votre annonce to the full closing formula.
days.paretoFocus: French cover letters are highly formulaic, so learning five blocks lets you produce a correct letter for any job.
drills: Write the opening sentence of a cover letter for a job you would like.
Rewrite three casual sentences about yourself in a formal register.
Copy out the closing formula three times until you can write it from memory.

items: How do you politely introduce a request in a formal letter?
Which tense makes a claim about your experience sound modest?
Give the standard sentence offering yourself for an interview.

activities.title: Rédiger une lettre de motivation
activities.intro: A French cover letter runs on fixed blocks. Suite à + noun states the reason for writing, je me permets de + infinitive softens the request, and the conditional keeps your claims modest. The letter always ends with a long courtesy formula.

items.hr: Suite à votre annonce parue cette semaine, je me permets de vous adresser ma candidature.
items.en: Following your advertisement published this week, I am writing to send you my application.
items.note: je me permets de + infinitif = polite request

items.hr: Actuellement assistant commercial, je souhaite évoluer vers un poste de chargé de clientèle.
items.en: Currently a sales assistant, I would like to move towards an account manager position.

items.hr: Mon expérience dans la vente me permettrait d'être rapidement opérationnel.
items.en: My experience in sales would allow me to be up and running quickly.

items.hr: Je me tiens à votre disposition pour un entretien.
items.en: I am available for an interview.

items.hr: Je vous prie d'agréer, Madame, Monsieur, l'expression de mes salutations distinguées.
items.en: Yours faithfully.
items.note: learn this closing formula as one block

activities.title: Formules de candidature
activities.intro: Practise the formal register expected in a written application.

questions.prompt: Which opening is appropriate in a French cover letter?
options: Suite à votre annonce, je me permets de vous adresser ma candidature.
Salut, je veux le poste que vous proposez.
Je vous écris parce que j'ai vraiment besoin d'argent.
Votre annonce m'a plu, donnez-moi le poste.
questions.answer: Suite à votre annonce, je me permets de vous adresser ma candidature.
questions.explanation: je me permets de + infinitive is the standard polite way to introduce a request in formal writing.

questions.prompt: Which formula correctly ends a formal application letter?
options: Je vous prie d'agréer, Madame, Monsieur, mes salutations distinguées.
À plus tard, et merci d'avance.
Je vous prie d'accepter mon amitié.
questions.answer: Je vous prie d'agréer, Madame, Monsieur, mes salutations distinguées.
questions.explanation: The closing repeats the form of address used at the top and is fixed word for word.

questions.prompt: Complete the availability sentence: 'Je me ___ à votre disposition pour un entretien.'
questions.explanation: se tenir à la disposition de quelqu'un is the fixed formula offering your availability.

questions.prompt: Complete the closing: 'Je vous ___ d'agréer, Madame, Monsieur, mes salutations distinguées.'
questions.explanation: prier quelqu'un de + infinitive is the formal verb used in courtesy formulas.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I would like to move towards a management position.'
questions.explanation: souhaiter + infinitive is more formal than vouloir, and évoluer vers describes a career move.

activities.title: Relire une lettre de motivation
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Help a friend fix the register of a cover letter using the formal formulas of this lesson.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai écrit ma lettre, tu peux la relire ?
lines.en: I've written my letter, can you read it over?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr. Ta première phrase est trop familière : commence par Suite à votre annonce.
lines.en: Of course. Your first sentence is too casual: start with Suite à votre annonce.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour demander un entretien, je mets quoi ?
lines.en: And to ask for an interview, what do I put?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Écris plutôt : je me tiens à votre disposition pour un entretien.
lines.en: Write this instead: I am available for an interview.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je termine par cordialement, ça suffit ?
lines.en: I'm ending with cordialement, is that enough?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pour une candidature, mieux vaut la formule complète avec je vous prie d'agréer.
lines.en: For an application, the full formula with je vous prie d'agréer is better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour parler de mon expérience ?
lines.en: And to talk about my experience?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Utilise le conditionnel : mon expérience me permettrait d'être vite opérationnel.
lines.en: Use the conditional: my experience would allow me to be up and running quickly.

days.title: L'entretien d'embauche : se présenter professionnellement
days.objective: You can present yourself in a job interview using quelqu'un de + adjective, je dirais que and avoir du mal à.
days.paretoFocus: The self-description frames used in interviews also work for any situation where you must describe your character convincingly.
drills: Write three sentences describing your character with quelqu'un de + adjective.
Prepare an answer about one weakness using avoir du mal à plus a sentence about improving.
Say aloud why a job you know would suit you, using Ce poste correspond à.

items: Does a woman say quelqu'un de motivé or quelqu'une de motivée?
Which tense of dire hedges an opinion politely?
How do you say 'I find it hard to say no'?

activities.title: Se présenter en entretien
activities.intro: In interviews French speakers say je suis quelqu'un de + masculine adjective, whatever their own gender, because quelqu'un is invariable. Je dirais que hedges an opinion, while avoir du mal à + infinitive is the polite way to admit a weakness.

items.hr: Je suis quelqu'un de rigoureux et de très organisé.
items.en: I'm someone thorough and very organised.

items.hr: J'ai cinq ans d'expérience dans la logistique.
items.en: I have five years of experience in logistics.

items.hr: Je dirais que mon principal point fort, c'est le travail en équipe.
items.en: I'd say my main strength is teamwork.

items.hr: Mon défaut ? J'ai du mal à déléguer, mais j'y travaille.
items.en: My weakness? I find it hard to delegate, but I'm working on it.

items.hr: Ce poste correspond exactement à ce que je recherche.
items.en: This position matches exactly what I'm looking for.

activities.title: Questions d'entretien
activities.intro: Practise describing your character and hedging your claims.

questions.prompt: A woman is describing herself. Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: quelqu'un is invariable and always takes de plus a masculine adjective, even when a woman is speaking about herself.

questions.prompt: Which answer presents an opinion in a modest, hedged way?
options: Je dirais que c'est mon point fort.
Je dis que c'est mon point fort.
J'ai dit que c'est mon point fort.
Je disais que c'est mon point fort.
questions.answer: Je dirais que c'est mon point fort.
questions.explanation: The conditional je dirais presents the statement as a personal view rather than a fact.

questions.prompt: Complete, meaning that you find it difficult: 'J'ai du ___ à dire non, mais je progresse.'
questions.explanation: avoir du mal à + infinitive means to find something hard to do.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition: 'J'ai trois ans d'expérience ___ la vente.'
questions.explanation: Use dans before a sector introduced by an article; en is used with a bare noun, as in en informatique.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'This position matches exactly what I am looking for.'
questions.explanation: correspondre à takes à, and ce que introduces the thing you are looking for.

activities.title: L'entretien
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A recruiter probes your strengths and weaknesses; describe your character, hedge politely, and own up to one difficulty.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Parlez-moi un peu de vous.
lines.en: Tell me a little about yourself.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis quelqu'un de curieux et de très organisé, avec quatre ans d'expérience dans la logistique.
lines.en: I'm someone curious and very organised, with four years of experience in logistics.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quel est votre principal point fort ?
lines.en: What is your main strength?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je dirais que c'est ma capacité à gérer les imprévus.
lines.en: I'd say it's my ability to handle unexpected problems.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et votre principal défaut ?
lines.en: And your main weakness?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai du mal à déléguer, mais je m'améliore depuis un an.
lines.en: I find it hard to delegate, but I've been improving for a year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourquoi ce poste vous intéresse-t-il ?
lines.en: Why does this position interest you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parce qu'il correspond exactement à ce que je recherche.
lines.en: Because it matches exactly what I'm looking for.

days.title: L'argent et le budget : gérer ses dépenses
days.objective: You can talk about saving and spending using mettre de côté, revenir à + price, en avoir pour and être à découvert.
days.paretoFocus: Prices and budgets come up constantly, and the French cost verbs are nothing like the English ones, so they must be learned as blocks.
drills: Write three sentences about what your weekly shopping comes to, using revenir à.
Report two recent purchases with en avoir pour + amount.
Set yourself a savings goal in one sentence using mettre de côté.

items: How do you say 'I put a hundred euros aside every month'?
What does j'en ai eu pour cent euros mean?
Which expression says your bank account is in the red?

activities.title: Gérer son budget
activities.intro: Money talk relies on set verbs. Mettre de l'argent de côté means to save up, revenir à + price means to come to a total, and the very common spoken en avoir pour + amount states what something cost you. Être à découvert means to be overdrawn.

items.hr: J'essaie de mettre cent euros de côté chaque mois.
items.en: I try to put a hundred euros aside every month.
items.note: mettre de côté = to save up

items.hr: Les courses me reviennent à environ soixante euros par semaine.
items.en: The shopping comes to about sixty euros a week.

items.hr: J'en ai eu pour deux cents euros chez le dentiste.
items.en: It cost me two hundred euros at the dentist's.
items.note: en avoir pour + somme, very common in speech

items.hr: Je dépense trop en livraisons et en abonnements.
items.en: I spend too much on deliveries and subscriptions.

items.hr: Ce mois-ci, je suis à découvert, il va falloir se serrer la ceinture.
items.en: This month I'm overdrawn, we'll have to tighten our belts.
items.note: être à découvert = to be overdrawn

activities.title: Dépenses et économies
activities.intro: Practise the everyday verbs of saving, spending and cost.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'I put money aside every month'?
options: Je mets de l'argent de côté tous les mois.
Je mets de l'argent au côté tous les mois.
Je pose de l'argent de côté tous les mois.
Je garde de l'argent en côté tous les mois.
questions.answer: Je mets de l'argent de côté tous les mois.
questions.explanation: The expression is mettre quelque chose de côté; the verb is mettre and the phrase de côté never changes.

questions.prompt: A friend says 'J'en ai eu pour trois cents euros.' What happened?
options: It cost him three hundred euros.
questions.answer: It cost him three hundred euros.
questions.explanation: en avoir pour + amount is the everyday way of stating what something cost you.

questions.prompt: Complete, meaning that they come to that total: 'Avec les enfants, les courses ___ à plus de cent euros par semaine.'
questions.explanation: revenir à + price expresses the final total that something works out at.

questions.prompt: Complete, meaning overdrawn: 'Je suis à ___ ce mois-ci, ma banque m'a envoyé un message.'
questions.explanation: être à découvert means your account has gone below zero.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I spend too much on subscriptions and deliveries.'
questions.explanation: dépenser en + category, and en is repeated before each item in the list.

activities.title: Faire le point sur le budget
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Review the monthly budget with a flatmate using revenir à, en avoir pour and mettre de côté.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On dépense beaucoup trop ce mois-ci, non ?
lines.en: We're spending far too much this month, aren't we?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui. Rien que les courses nous reviennent à deux cent quarante euros.
lines.en: Yes. The shopping alone comes to two hundred and forty euros.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et l'électricité ?
lines.en: And the electricity?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'en ai eu pour quatre-vingts euros, c'est plus que d'habitude.
lines.en: It cost me eighty euros, which is more than usual.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce qu'on peut réduire ?
lines.en: What can we cut back on?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: On dépense trop en livraisons. Si on cuisine plus, on peut mettre cent euros de côté.
lines.en: We spend too much on deliveries. If we cook more, we can put a hundred euros aside.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord, on essaie pendant un mois.
lines.en: All right, let's try for a month.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, sinon on va vite se retrouver à découvert.
lines.en: Perfect, otherwise we'll quickly end up overdrawn.

days.title: La technologie et les réseaux sociaux : usages et limites
days.objective: You can discuss digital habits and their risks using permettre de, passer du temps à, à force de and le risque, c'est de.
days.paretoFocus: These four frames let you state what a tool does, how long you use it and what could go wrong, the core of any modern discussion.
drills: Write three sentences saying what an app you use lets you do, with permettre de.
Estimate how long you spend on three activities using passer du temps à.
State two downsides of social media, each framed as a risk of doing something.

items: Which preposition follows passer une heure before an infinitive?
What does à force de + infinitive express?
How do you say 'the risk is believing everything you read'?

activities.title: Usages et limites du numérique
activities.intro: To talk about technology you need frames for function and for risk: permettre de + infinitive says what a tool lets you do, passer du temps à + infinitive says how long you spend, and the danger is named with le risque followed by c'est de + infinitive. À force de + infinitive describes a result produced by repetition.

items.hr: Les réseaux sociaux permettent de garder le contact avec sa famille.
items.en: Social media makes it possible to stay in touch with your family.

items.hr: Je passe au moins deux heures par jour à regarder mon téléphone.
items.en: I spend at least two hours a day looking at my phone.

items.hr: Le risque, c'est de croire tout ce qu'on lit en ligne.
items.en: The risk is believing everything you read online.

items.hr: À force de tout partager, on finit par ne plus avoir de vie privée.
items.en: By sharing everything all the time, you end up with no private life left.
items.note: à force de + infinitif, finir par + infinitif

items.hr: J'ai désactivé les notifications pour être moins dérangé.
items.en: I turned off notifications so as to be less disturbed.

activities.title: Écrans et réseaux
activities.intro: Practise saying what tools allow, how long you use them and what the risks are.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct verb: 'Cette application ___ de traduire hors connexion.'
questions.explanation: permettre de + infinitive is the standard way to say what a tool makes possible.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'I spend an hour a day scrolling through my feed'?
options: Je passe une heure par jour à faire défiler mon fil.
Je passe une heure par jour de faire défiler mon fil.
Je dépense une heure par jour à faire défiler mon fil.
Je prends une heure par jour pour défiler mon fil.
questions.answer: Je passe une heure par jour à faire défiler mon fil.
questions.explanation: passer du temps takes à before the infinitive, and dépenser is only used for money.

questions.prompt: Complete, expressing a result of doing it constantly: 'À ___ de comparer sa vie à celle des autres, on devient malheureux.'
questions.explanation: à force de + infinitive expresses a consequence produced by repeated action.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition: 'Le risque, c'est ___ perdre le contact avec la réalité.'
questions.explanation: After c'est an infinitive needs de in this frame: le risque, c'est de + infinitive.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I turned off notifications in order to work better.'
questions.explanation: pour + infinitive expresses purpose when both clauses share the same subject, and mieux comes before the infinitive.

activities.title: Trop de temps devant l'écran
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Compare screen time with a friend: what your phone lets you do, and what worries you about it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as vu ton temps d'écran cette semaine ?
lines.en: Have you seen your screen time this week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je passe presque trois heures par jour à regarder mon téléphone.
lines.en: Yes, I spend nearly three hours a day looking at my phone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est énorme. Pourquoi tu ne supprimes pas les applications ?
lines.en: That's huge. Why don't you delete the apps?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parce que ça me permet aussi de suivre l'actualité et de parler à ma sœur.
lines.en: Because it also lets me follow the news and talk to my sister.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y a quand même un risque, non ?
lines.en: There's still a risk, isn't there?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr, le risque, c'est de croire tout ce qu'on lit.
lines.en: Of course, the risk is believing everything you read.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as changé quelque chose ?
lines.en: Have you changed anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai désactivé les notifications, et je suis beaucoup moins dérangé.
lines.en: I turned off notifications, and I'm much less disturbed.

days.title: Les loisirs et la culture : parler de ses pratiques
days.objective: You can describe your hobbies and how long you have practised them using faire de + article, ça fait ... que and the negative ne ... plus de.
days.paretoFocus: Hobbies are the first thing people ask about socially, and the partitive after faire is a mistake learners repeat for years.
drills: List four activities you do or used to do, each with the correct form of faire de.
Write two sentences with ça fait ... que about things you still do today.
Say what you have given up and why, using ne fais plus de.

items: Is it je fais de la guitare or je fais la guitare?
Which tense follows ça fait cinq ans que?
What happens to de la after a negation?

activities.title: Parler de ses pratiques culturelles
activities.intro: Activities take faire plus a partitive article that agrees with the noun: faire du sport, faire de la photo, faire de l'escalade. Duration uses ça fait + time + que with the present tense, and after a negation the partitive shrinks to plain de.

items.hr: Je fais de la photo depuis l'adolescence.
items.en: I've been doing photography since my teens.

items.hr: Ça fait cinq ans que je chante dans une chorale.
items.en: I've been singing in a choir for five years.
items.note: ça fait X que + présent for an ongoing action

items.hr: Je ne fais plus de théâtre, faute de temps.
items.en: I don't do theatre any more, for lack of time.

items.hr: Je vais parfois au musée le dimanche, quand l'entrée est gratuite.
items.en: I sometimes go to the museum on Sundays, when entry is free.

items.hr: Je suis plutôt cinéma que série, je préfère voir un film en entier.
items.en: I'm more of a cinema person than a series person, I prefer watching a whole film.
items.note: être plutôt X que Y, informal preference

activities.title: Loisirs et sorties
activities.intro: Practise the partitive after faire and ways of stating how long you have done something.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Je fais ___ guitare depuis deux ans.'
questions.explanation: faire takes a partitive article agreeing with the noun, and la guitare gives de la guitare.

questions.prompt: Which sentence says you have been painting for three years and still are?
options: Ça fait trois ans que je peins.
Il y a trois ans que je peignais.
Ça fait trois ans que j'ai peint.
questions.answer: Ça fait trois ans que je peins.
questions.explanation: ça fait + duration + que takes the present tense when the action is still going on.

questions.prompt: Complete the negative sentence: 'Je ne fais plus ___ danse cette année.'
questions.explanation: After a negation the partitive de la is reduced to plain de.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ça ___ deux ans que je prends des cours de dessin.'
questions.explanation: The impersonal ça fait + duration + que states how long something has been going on.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I sometimes go to the museum when entry is free.'
questions.explanation: aller à + le contracts to au, and parfois usually sits just after the conjugated verb.

activities.title: Le temps libre
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Talk about your hobbies using faire de + article and ça fait ... que.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu fais quoi de tes week-ends, en ce moment ?
lines.en: What do you do with your weekends at the moment?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je fais de la photo. Ça fait presque dix ans que j'en fais.
lines.en: I do photography. I've been doing it for nearly ten years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu ne faisais pas du théâtre avant ?
lines.en: Didn't you use to do theatre?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si, mais je n'en fais plus, faute de temps.
lines.en: Yes, but I don't do it any more, for lack of time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et les sorties culturelles ?
lines.en: And cultural outings?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vais parfois au musée le dimanche, quand l'entrée est gratuite.
lines.en: I sometimes go to the museum on Sundays, when entry is free.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cinéma ou séries ?
lines.en: Cinema or series?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis plutôt cinéma, je préfère voir un film en entier.
lines.en: I'm more of a cinema person, I prefer watching a whole film.

days.title: Le bénévolat et la solidarité
days.objective: You can talk about volunteering using s'engager dans, donner un coup de main, en tant que and purpose clauses with pour que plus the subjunctive.
days.paretoFocus: When the goal of an action belongs to somebody else, French switches to a purpose clause in the subjunctive, a pattern useful far beyond this topic.
drills: Write three sentences about causes you would support, each with s'engager dans.
Write two purpose sentences with pour que and check the subjunctive form.
Describe a role you could play using en tant que plus a noun without an article.

items: When do you use pour que rather than pour plus an infinitive?
How do you say 'to lend a hand' in French?
Which article follows en tant que?

activities.title: S'engager comme bénévole
activities.intro: Volunteering language turns on s'engager dans + organisation, the coup de main idiom for lending a hand, and en tant que + role with no article. For purpose, use pour + infinitive when the subject stays the same, and pour que + subjunctive when it changes.

items.hr: Je me suis engagé dans une association de quartier.
items.en: I got involved in a local community association.

items.hr: Je donne un coup de main à la distribution alimentaire le samedi.
items.en: I lend a hand at the food distribution on Saturdays.

items.hr: En tant que bénévole, je n'ai aucune obligation de résultat.
items.en: As a volunteer, I have no obligation to deliver results.
items.note: en tant que + rôle, sans article

items.hr: On récolte des vêtements pour que les familles passent un hiver correct.
items.en: We collect clothes so that families can get through the winter decently.
items.note: pour que + subjonctif, deux sujets différents

items.hr: Deux heures par semaine, ça peut vraiment changer les choses.
items.en: Two hours a week can really make a difference.

activities.title: Donner de son temps
activities.intro: Practise purpose clauses and the vocabulary of voluntary work.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct link: 'On organise une collecte ___ tout le monde ait de quoi manger.'
questions.explanation: When the two clauses have different subjects you need pour que plus the subjunctive; pour and afin de take an infinitive with a shared subject.

questions.prompt: Which phrase means 'as a volunteer', stating the capacity you act in?
questions.explanation: en tant que is followed directly by the role, with no article.

questions.prompt: Put the verb in the right mood: 'Nous récoltons des jouets pour que les enfants (avoir) ___ un cadeau.'
questions.explanation: pour que always triggers the subjunctive, and avoir gives qu'ils aient.

questions.prompt: Complete, meaning to lend a hand: 'Je donne un coup de ___ à l'association tous les samedis.'
questions.explanation: donner un coup de main is the everyday expression for helping out.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I got involved in a local association last year.'
questions.explanation: s'engager is a reflexive verb, so it uses être in the passé composé and the participle agrees with the subject.

activities.title: Devenir bénévole
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Offer your time to an association, stating your role and what you hope your help will make possible.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu cherches à t'engager quelque part ?
lines.en: Are you looking to get involved somewhere?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je voudrais donner un coup de main deux heures par semaine.
lines.en: Yes, I'd like to lend a hand two hours a week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nous avons besoin de monde à la distribution alimentaire.
lines.en: We need people at the food distribution.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En tant que bénévole, qu'est-ce qu'on me demanderait exactement ?
lines.en: As a volunteer, what exactly would I be asked to do?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Préparer les colis et accueillir les familles.
lines.en: Prepare the parcels and welcome the families.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ça me va. On fait ça pour que les familles passent un hiver correct, c'est bien ça ?
lines.en: That suits me. We do it so that families get through the winter decently, is that right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exactement. Tu peux commencer samedi ?
lines.en: Exactly. Can you start on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Samedi prochain, sans problème.
lines.en: Next Saturday, no problem.

days.title: La famille moderne et les relations
days.objective: You can describe a blended family and how you get on with its members using s'entendre avec, se disputer and the beau- and demi- prefixes.
days.paretoFocus: Reciprocal verbs plus the beau- and demi- system cover most family talk, and beau-père has two meanings you must be able to tell apart.
drills: Draw your family tree and label each person in French.
Write three sentences about who you get on with, using s'entendre bien or mal.
Explain in two sentences how a relationship has improved, starting with Depuis qu'on.

items: What are the two possible meanings of beau-père?
How do you say 'my sister and I get on well'?
Which prefix marks a sibling with only one parent in common?

activities.title: La famille et les relations
activities.intro: Relationships use reciprocal reflexive verbs: s'entendre bien avec, se disputer, se voir. Family words built with beau- indicate a link created by marriage, while demi- marks a sibling sharing only one parent.

items.hr: Je m'entends très bien avec ma belle-mère.
items.en: I get on very well with my stepmother.

items.hr: Mes parents ont divorcé quand j'avais dix ans.
items.en: My parents got divorced when I was ten.

items.hr: J'ai une demi-sœur de huit ans et un beau-frère très sympa.
items.en: I have an eight-year-old half-sister and a very nice brother-in-law.
items.note: demi- = un parent commun, beau- = par alliance

items.hr: On se dispute rarement, et quand ça arrive, ça ne dure pas.
items.en: We rarely argue, and when it happens, it doesn't last.

items.hr: Depuis qu'ils vivent ensemble, ça se passe beaucoup mieux entre eux.
items.en: Since they started living together, things have been going much better between them.
items.note: ça se passe bien entre X et Y

activities.title: Liens familiaux
activities.intro: Practise family vocabulary and reciprocal verbs.

questions.prompt: Your friend's mother has remarried. Her new husband is, for your friend, a ___.
questions.explanation: beau- covers relations created by marriage, so beau-père means both stepfather and father-in-law depending on context.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'my sister and I get on well'?
options: Ma sœur et moi, on s'entend bien.
Ma sœur et moi, on entend bien.
Ma sœur et moi, on s'écoute bien.
Ma sœur et moi, on nous entend bien.
questions.answer: Ma sœur et moi, on s'entend bien.
questions.explanation: s'entendre is reciprocal and reflexive; with on the pronoun is se and the verb stays singular.

questions.prompt: Complete, meaning that you almost never argue: 'Nous ne nous ___ presque jamais, même sur la politique.'
questions.explanation: se disputer is reciprocal, so the pronoun repeats the subject: nous nous disputons.

questions.prompt: Complete with the prefix showing that you share only one parent: 'Ma mère s'est remariée, et j'ai une ___-sœur de six ans.'
questions.explanation: demi- marks a sibling with one parent in common, unlike beau- which marks a link by marriage.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Things have been going much better between them since last year.'
questions.explanation: ça se passe bien is the impersonal way to describe how a relationship is going, and depuis + a time phrase needs no que.

activities.title: Une famille recomposée
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Describe your blended family to a colleague using s'entendre avec, demi- and beau-.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as des frères et sœurs ?
lines.en: Do you have brothers and sisters?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un frère, et une demi-sœur de douze ans, du côté de mon père.
lines.en: A brother, and a twelve-year-old half-sister on my father's side.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ça se passe bien avec la nouvelle famille ?
lines.en: And is it going well with the new family?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Très bien. Je m'entends beaucoup mieux avec ma belle-mère qu'au début.
lines.en: Very well. I get on much better with my stepmother than at the beginning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous vous voyez souvent ?
lines.en: Do you see each other often?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Une fois par mois. On se dispute rarement, maintenant.
lines.en: Once a month. We rarely argue now.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est plutôt rare, ça.
lines.en: That's fairly rare.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, mais depuis qu'on habite dans la même ville, tout est plus simple.
lines.en: Yes, but since we've been living in the same town, everything is simpler.

days.title: Les démarches administratives : comprendre un dossier
days.objective: You can understand and respond to an administrative file, using il faut que plus the subjunctive, veuillez plus an infinitive and key nouns like justificatif and délai.
days.paretoFocus: Administrative French is impersonal and formulaic, so a handful of nouns and two structures unlock nearly every form you will meet.
drills: List five documents you might be asked for and write each with its French name.
Rewrite three instructions as veuillez plus an infinitive.
Write two obligations for yourself with il faut que and check the subjunctive.

items: Which mood follows il faut que?
What is un justificatif de domicile?
Does un délai mean a delay or a time allowed?

activities.title: Comprendre un dossier administratif
activities.intro: Administrative French is impersonal: it uses the passive (est exigé), veuillez plus an infinitive for polite orders, and the impersonal il manque for what is missing. Obligation addressed to you is expressed with il faut que and a subjunctive verb.

items.hr: Un justificatif de domicile de moins de trois mois est exigé.
items.en: Proof of address less than three months old is required.
items.note: être exigé, passif typique des formulaires

items.hr: Veuillez joindre une copie de votre pièce d'identité.
items.en: Please attach a copy of your identity document.

items.hr: Il faut que vous remplissiez le formulaire en majuscules.
items.en: You must fill in the form in capital letters.
items.note: il faut que + subjonctif: remplir donne remplissiez

items.hr: Votre dossier est incomplet : il manque une attestation.
items.en: Your file is incomplete: an official statement is missing.

items.hr: Le délai de traitement est de six à huit semaines.
items.en: The processing time is six to eight weeks.
items.note: un délai = time allowed, not a delay

activities.title: Au guichet
activities.intro: Practise reading form instructions and expressing administrative obligations.

questions.prompt: A form says 'Veuillez joindre un justificatif de domicile.' What must you do?
options: Show your passport at the counter.
Sign in front of an official.
questions.explanation: un justificatif de domicile proves where you live, and veuillez plus an infinitive is a formal instruction.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct mood: 'Il faut que vous ___ le formulaire avant vendredi.'
questions.explanation: il faut que always takes the subjunctive, and remplir gives que vous remplissiez.

questions.prompt: Complete, saying what is missing: 'Votre dossier est incomplet, il ___ une attestation d'assurance.'
questions.explanation: The impersonal il manque plus a noun states what has not been provided.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun meaning processing time: 'Le ___ de traitement est de six semaines.'
questions.explanation: un délai is the period allowed or needed, and it is a false friend of the English word delay.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Proof of address is required for this application.'
questions.explanation: The passive être plus past participle is standard in official texts because it hides who is asking.

activities.title: Déposer un dossier
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A clerk tells you your dossier is missing a document; find out which one and how long you have.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, votre dossier est incomplet.
lines.en: Hello, your file is incomplete.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ah bon ? Qu'est-ce qu'il manque exactement ?
lines.en: Really? What exactly is missing?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Un justificatif de domicile de moins de trois mois.
lines.en: Proof of address less than three months old.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Une facture d'électricité de mai, ça convient ?
lines.en: Would an electricity bill from May do?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, mais il faut que vous joigniez aussi une copie de votre pièce d'identité.
lines.en: Yes, but you also have to attach a copy of your identity document.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je peux revenir cet après-midi avec les deux documents.
lines.en: I can come back this afternoon with both documents.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Très bien. Comptez ensuite six à huit semaines de traitement.
lines.en: Very well. After that, allow six to eight weeks for processing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord, merci pour ces précisions.
lines.en: All right, thank you for these details.

days.title: La publicité et la consommation
days.objective: You can discuss advertising and buying decisions using inciter à, faire croire que, se laisser tenter and the contrast between besoin and envie.
days.paretoFocus: The verbs of influence are the same ones used to talk about politics, media and persuasion of any kind.
drills: Write three sentences about what advertising encourages people to do, using inciter à.
Describe a purchase you regret with se laisser tenter.
Sort five recent purchases into besoin and envie and justify each in one sentence.

items: Which preposition follows inciter quelqu'un?
Which auxiliary does se laisser tenter take in the past?
What is the difference between un besoin and une envie?

activities.title: Publicité et consommation
activities.intro: Advertising language is about influence: inciter quelqu'un à + infinitive, and faire croire que + indicative. To describe giving in, French uses se laisser + infinitive, which takes être in the past. Finally, un besoin and une envie are not the same thing.

items.hr: Les publicités incitent les jeunes à acheter toujours plus.
items.en: Advertisements encourage young people to buy more and more.

items.hr: Cette marque nous fait croire que son produit est indispensable.
items.en: This brand makes us believe its product is essential.

items.hr: Je me suis laissé tenter par une promotion.
items.en: I let myself be tempted by a special offer.

items.hr: Ce n'est pas un besoin, c'est une envie.
items.en: It isn't a need, it's a want.
items.note: avoir besoin de vs avoir envie de

items.hr: Avant d'acheter, je compare les prix et je lis les avis.
items.en: Before buying, I compare prices and read the reviews.

activities.title: Résister aux promotions
activities.intro: Practise the verbs of influence and the language of buying decisions.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'La publicité incite les enfants ___ réclamer des jouets.'
questions.explanation: inciter quelqu'un à plus an infinitive; the preposition never changes.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'I gave in to a special offer'?
options: Je me suis laissé tenter par une promotion.
J'ai laissé me tenter par une promotion.
Je me suis laissé de tenter par une promotion.
Je suis laissé tenter par une promotion.
questions.answer: Je me suis laissé tenter par une promotion.
questions.explanation: se laisser plus a bare infinitive means to let something happen to you, and the reflexive form takes être.

questions.prompt: Complete, meaning to make us believe: 'Cette marque nous fait ___ que nous ne pouvons pas vivre sans son produit.'
questions.explanation: faire croire que plus the indicative means to make someone believe something.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition: 'Avant ___ signer, je lis toujours le contrat.'
questions.explanation: avant is followed by de plus an infinitive when the subject is the same in both parts.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Advertising encourages us to consume more.'
questions.explanation: The object pronoun goes before the conjugated verb, and inciter keeps à before the infinitive.

activities.title: Une promotion irrésistible
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Discuss a purchase with a friend using se laisser tenter, inciter à and besoin versus envie.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as encore acheté un nouveau téléphone ?
lines.en: Have you bought yet another new phone?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je me suis laissé tenter par une promotion.
lines.en: Yes, I let myself be tempted by a special offer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu en avais vraiment besoin ?
lines.en: Did you really need it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Honnêtement, non. C'était une envie, pas un besoin.
lines.en: Honestly, no. It was a want, not a need.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La publicité fait bien son travail.
lines.en: Advertising is doing its job well.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est vrai, elle nous incite à changer d'appareil tous les deux ans.
lines.en: It's true, it encourages us to change devices every two years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu vas revendre l'ancien ?
lines.en: Are you going to sell the old one?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, et la prochaine fois, je comparerai les prix avant de commander.
lines.en: Yes, and next time I'll compare prices before ordering.

days.title: Les faits divers : comprendre un court article
days.objective: You can read and retell a short news item, recognising the passive, attributions with selon and the conditional used for unconfirmed facts.
days.paretoFocus: Three press features, passive, selon and the conditional, explain most of what makes short French articles look difficult.
drills: Rewrite three active sentences in the passive with être plus a past participle.
Report two pieces of news you are unsure about, using the conditional.
Summarise a short news item you know in four French sentences.

items: What does the conditional signal in a news report?
How do you say 'according to the police'?
Give the French for 'an investigation has been opened'.

activities.title: Lire un fait divers
activities.intro: Short news items rely on three features. The passive (être plus past participle) hides who did what, selon plus a source attributes information, and the conditional signals that a fact has not been confirmed. Recognising them makes such articles far easier.

items.hr: Un homme de trente ans a été interpellé hier soir.
items.en: A thirty-year-old man was arrested yesterday evening.

items.hr: Selon la police, il n'y a pas eu de blessés.
items.en: According to the police, there were no injuries.

items.hr: L'incendie aurait été provoqué par un court-circuit.
items.en: The fire was apparently caused by a short circuit.

items.hr: Une enquête a été ouverte pour déterminer les causes de l'accident.
items.en: An investigation has been opened to determine the causes of the accident.

items.hr: Le conducteur, légèrement blessé, a été transporté à l'hôpital.
items.en: The driver, slightly injured, was taken to hospital.

activities.title: Comprendre un article court
activities.intro: Practise decoding the passive, attributions and unconfirmed information in the press.

questions.prompt: An article says 'L'incendie aurait été provoqué par un court-circuit.' What does the conditional signal?
questions.explanation: In the press the conditional marks information that has been reported but not verified.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct passive: 'Deux personnes ___ légèrement blessées.'
questions.explanation: The passive in the passé composé is avoir plus été plus a past participle that agrees with the subject.

questions.prompt: Complete the attribution, meaning according to: '___ les pompiers, le feu a été maîtrisé en une heure.'
questions.explanation: selon plus a source introduces reported information and needs no article or preposition.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun used when the police start investigating: 'Une ___ a été ouverte pour déterminer les causes du drame.'
questions.explanation: ouvrir une enquête is the standard collocation in French news reports.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'A man was arrested yesterday evening.'
questions.explanation: interpeller is the press verb for arresting someone, and the passive keeps the agent unnamed.

activities.title: Commenter un fait divers
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Report a short news item to a friend using the passive, selon and the conditional.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as lu ce qui s'est passé dans le centre hier ?
lines.en: Did you read what happened in the town centre yesterday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, un magasin a été touché par un incendie vers vingt heures.
lines.en: Yes, a shop was hit by a fire at around eight in the evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y a eu des blessés ?
lines.en: Were there any injuries?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Selon les pompiers, personne n'a été blessé.
lines.en: According to the fire brigade, nobody was hurt.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On sait ce qui a déclenché le feu ?
lines.en: Do we know what started the fire?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'incendie aurait été provoqué par un court-circuit, mais rien n'est confirmé.
lines.en: The fire was apparently caused by a short circuit, but nothing is confirmed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et maintenant ?
lines.en: And what now?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Une enquête a été ouverte pour déterminer les causes exactes.
lines.en: An investigation has been opened to determine the exact causes.

days.title: La francophonie : le français dans le monde
days.objective: You can describe where French is spoken, give approximate figures and mention regional differences in vocabulary and accent.
days.paretoFocus: Approximation words like une trentaine, la plupart de and selon let you talk about facts and statistics without needing exact numbers, which comes up in almost every discussion.
drills: Say aloud three facts about French in the world, each using an approximation word.
Write two sentences comparing how a word is said in two different French speaking regions.
Add the approximation expressions from this lesson to your flashcards and review them once.

items: How do you say 'about thirty countries' in French?
Which verb form follows 'la plupart des francophones'?
Give the French phrase meaning 'depending on the region'.

activities.title: Parler du français dans le monde
activities.intro: French is an official language in dozens of countries, so you often need to speak in approximate terms. Nouns like une trentaine give a rough number, and la plupart de takes a plural verb when it is followed by a plural noun. Selon plus a noun means 'depending on'.

items.hr: Le français est une langue officielle dans une trentaine de pays.
items.en: French is an official language in about thirty countries.

items.hr: La plupart des francophones vivent aujourd'hui en Afrique.
items.en: Most French speakers live in Africa today.
items.note: la plupart des + plural noun takes a plural verb

items.hr: Le vocabulaire et l'accent changent beaucoup selon les régions.
items.en: Vocabulary and accent change a lot depending on the region.

items.hr: Au Québec, on entend souvent le verbe magasiner pour faire les courses.
items.en: In Quebec, you often hear the verb magasiner for going shopping.
items.note: regional verbs exist alongside standard ones

items.hr: Environ un francophone sur cinq apprend le français comme langue étrangère.
items.en: About one French speaker in five learns French as a foreign language.
items.note: un ... sur cinq = one in five

activities.title: Chiffres et proportions
activities.intro: Practise approximation words and the agreement that follows la plupart.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct completion: 'La plupart des étudiants ___ deux langues.'
questions.explanation: La plupart des plus a plural noun controls the verb, so the verb is plural: parlent.

questions.prompt: Which French expression means 'about forty'?
questions.explanation: Adding the ending -aine to a round number makes an approximation noun: une quarantaine de personnes.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Le vocabulaire change ___ les pays.' (meaning: depending on)
questions.explanation: Selon plus a noun expresses variation: selon les pays, selon les régions.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Une ___ de pays ont le français comme langue officielle.' (about thirty)
questions.explanation: Trente becomes trentaine to give an approximate quantity.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Most French speakers live in Africa.'
questions.explanation: Subject expression first, then the plural verb vivent, then the place with en for feminine continents.

activities.title: Une rencontre entre francophones
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You meet someone at a language meet-up and talk about where French is spoken, using approximation words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu savais que le français est parlé sur les cinq continents ?
lines.en: Did you know that French is spoken on five continents?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, dans une trentaine de pays, si je me souviens bien.
lines.en: Yes, in about thirty countries, if I remember correctly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la plupart des locuteurs sont en Europe, non ?
lines.en: And most speakers are in Europe, right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, la plupart des francophones vivent en Afrique aujourd'hui.
lines.en: No, most French speakers live in Africa today.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Est-ce que tout le monde parle de la même façon ?
lines.en: Does everyone speak the same way?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas vraiment : l'accent et le vocabulaire changent selon les régions.
lines.en: Not really: the accent and vocabulary change depending on the region.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as un exemple de mot différent ?
lines.en: Do you have an example of a different word?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Au Québec, on dit magasiner, et environ un mot sur dix me surprend encore.
lines.en: In Quebec, people say magasiner, and about one word in ten still surprises me.

days.title: Vivre à l'étranger : s'installer et s'adapter
days.objective: You can say how long you have been somewhere, describe what you miss and explain what you are getting used to.
days.paretoFocus: Manquer works backwards compared to English, and getting it wrong changes who misses whom, so fixing it once pays off in every personal conversation.
drills: Say three things you would miss if you moved abroad, using manquer correctly.
Write two sentences with ça fait ... que about your own life.
List three things a newcomer has to get used to in your town and say them with s'habituer à.

items: How do you say 'I miss my family' in French?
Which structure means 'I have trouble doing something'?
Give the French for 'I have been living here for six months'.

activities.title: S'installer dans un nouveau pays
activities.intro: Manquer reverses the roles: the person or thing that is missed becomes the subject. Ça fait plus a duration plus que uses the present tense for something still true. S'habituer à and avoir du mal à describe adapting.

items.hr: Ça fait six mois que je vis à l'étranger.
items.en: I have been living abroad for six months.

items.hr: Ma famille me manque beaucoup.
items.en: I miss my family a lot.
items.note: the missed person is the subject of manquer

items.hr: Je commence à m'habituer aux horaires d'ici.
items.en: I am starting to get used to the timetable here.
items.note: s'habituer à + noun, à + les becomes aux

items.hr: Au début, j'avais du mal à comprendre les gens dans la rue.
items.en: At first, I had trouble understanding people in the street.

items.hr: Petit à petit, je me sens chez moi ici.
items.en: Little by little, I feel at home here.
items.note: se sentir chez soi = to feel at home

activities.title: S'adapter, jour après jour
activities.intro: Practise manquer, s'habituer à and duration with ça fait.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'I miss my friends'?
questions.explanation: With manquer the people missed are the subject and the person feeling the loss is an indirect object pronoun.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Je m'habitue ___ climat humide.'
questions.explanation: S'habituer always takes à, and à plus le contracts to au.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'J'ai du ___ à comprendre l'accent d'ici.' (meaning: I find it hard)
questions.explanation: Avoir du mal à plus an infinitive is the standard way to say you struggle with something.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Ça ___ deux ans que j'habite à Lyon.' (meaning: it has been two years)
questions.explanation: The set phrase is ça fait plus duration plus que, and the following verb stays in the present.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Little by little, I feel at home here.'
questions.explanation: The time expression opens the sentence, then the reflexive verb se sentir, then chez moi for at home.

activities.title: Deux mois après le déménagement
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour asks how your move abroad is going, and you answer with manquer, s'habituer à and avoir du mal à.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, ça fait combien de temps que tu es ici ?
lines.en: So, how long have you been here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ça fait deux mois que je suis installé dans le quartier.
lines.en: I have been settled in the neighbourhood for two months.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le rythme de vie, tu t'y habitues ?
lines.en: And the pace of life, are you getting used to it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je m'habitue aux horaires, mais j'ai encore du mal à suivre les conversations rapides.
lines.en: I am getting used to the timetable, but I still have trouble following fast conversations.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce qui te manque le plus ?
lines.en: What do you miss the most?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mes amis me manquent, surtout les dimanches.
lines.en: I miss my friends, especially on Sundays.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ça va venir, tu verras.
lines.en: It will come, you will see.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, petit à petit je me sens chez moi ici.
lines.en: Yes, little by little I feel at home here.

days.title: Le tourisme et les vacances responsables
days.objective: You can recommend one travel option over another and explain the impact of mass tourism.
days.paretoFocus: Il vaut mieux, éviter de and plutôt que de are the three quickest ways to give advice and state a preference without sounding blunt.
drills: Give three pieces of travel advice, each starting with il vaut mieux.
Write two sentences contrasting a choice, using plutôt que de plus an infinitive.
Say aloud two problems caused by mass tourism in a place you know.

items: Which word follows il vaut mieux: an infinitive or a conjugated verb?
How do you say 'rather than booking a hotel' in French?
Give the French verb meaning 'to avoid'.

activities.title: Voyager autrement
activities.intro: Il vaut mieux is impersonal and is followed directly by an infinitive. Plutôt que de plus an infinitive contrasts two actions. Devrait and devraient are conditional forms of devoir and mean should.

items.hr: Il vaut mieux prendre le train que l'avion pour les courts trajets.
items.en: It is better to take the train than the plane for short journeys.
items.note: il vaut mieux + infinitive, no preposition

items.hr: J'essaie d'éviter les endroits trop touristiques en plein été.
items.en: I try to avoid places that are too touristy in the middle of summer.
items.note: éviter + noun, or éviter de + infinitive

items.hr: Nous avons choisi une chambre chez l'habitant plutôt que de réserver un grand hôtel.
items.en: We chose a room in someone's home rather than booking a big hotel.

items.hr: Le tourisme de masse abîme peu à peu les sites naturels.
items.en: Mass tourism slowly damages natural sites.

items.hr: Les voyageurs devraient respecter les sentiers balisés.
items.en: Travellers should stay on the marked paths.

activities.title: Conseils de voyage
activities.intro: Practise giving travel advice and contrasting two options.

questions.prompt: Which sentence gives advice correctly?
options: Il vaut mieux réserver à l'avance.
Il vaut mieux réservant à l'avance.
Il vaut mieux que réserver à l'avance.
Il vaut mieux de réserver à l'avance.
questions.answer: Il vaut mieux réserver à l'avance.
questions.explanation: Il vaut mieux is followed by a bare infinitive, with no de and no que.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct word: 'Nous marchons ___ que de prendre la voiture.'
questions.explanation: Plutôt que de plus an infinitive means rather than doing something; mieux compares how well an action is done.

questions.prompt: Complete with one verb: 'J'essaie d'___ les plages bondées au mois d'août.' (meaning: to keep away from)
questions.explanation: Éviter takes a direct object when it is followed by a noun, so no preposition after it here.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Les touristes ___ respecter les règles locales.' (should, conditional of devoir, third person plural)
questions.explanation: The conditional of devoir gives soft advice, and the ils form is devraient.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Mass tourism slowly damages natural sites.'
questions.explanation: The subject group comes first, then the verb, then the adverbial phrase, then the object.

activities.title: Préparer un voyage responsable
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is planning a summer trip and you suggest more responsible choices with il vaut mieux and plutôt que de.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On prend l'avion pour aller sur la côte ?
lines.en: Shall we fly to the coast?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il vaut mieux prendre le train, le trajet dure à peine quatre heures.
lines.en: It is better to take the train, the journey barely lasts four hours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord. Et pour dormir, un hôtel au bord de la mer ?
lines.en: All right. And for sleeping, a hotel by the sea?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: On pourrait loger chez l'habitant plutôt que de réserver un grand hôtel.
lines.en: We could stay in someone's home rather than booking a big hotel.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu crois vraiment que ça change quelque chose ?
lines.en: Do you really think it makes a difference?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, le tourisme de masse abîme les sites, donc j'essaie d'éviter les endroits saturés.
lines.en: Yes, mass tourism damages the sites, so I try to avoid overcrowded places.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bon, tu me convaincs. On part quand ?
lines.en: Fine, you are convincing me. When do we leave?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En juin : les voyageurs devraient éviter le plein mois d'août.
lines.en: In June: travellers should avoid the middle of August.

days.title: Le climat et les gestes du quotidien
days.objective: You can describe small everyday habits that reduce your impact and say whether a trend is increasing or decreasing.
days.paretoFocus: De plus en plus and de moins en moins let you describe any trend in one phrase, and an infinitive used as a subject is how French states general habits.
drills: Describe three habits you have changed, using depuis plus a time period.
Write two sentences with de moins en moins de plus a noun.
Start three sentences with an infinitive used as the subject, such as trier ses déchets.

items: How do you say 'less and less water' in French?
Which tense goes with depuis for something still true?
Give the French for 'sorting your waste takes five minutes'.

activities.title: Les petits gestes qui comptent
activities.intro: An infinitive can act as the subject of a sentence and then takes a singular verb. Depuis plus a duration uses the present tense when the situation continues. De moins en moins de and de plus en plus de describe a trend.

items.hr: Nous consommons de moins en moins d'eau chaude.
items.en: We use less and less hot water.
items.note: de moins en moins de + noun, no article

items.hr: Trier ses déchets prend cinq minutes par jour.
items.en: Sorting your waste takes five minutes a day.

items.hr: Je me déplace surtout à vélo depuis un an.
items.en: I have mostly been getting around by bike for a year.

items.hr: Baisser le chauffage d'un degré réduit vraiment la facture.
items.en: Turning the heating down by one degree really cuts the bill.

items.hr: Ce sont les petits gestes répétés qui comptent le plus.
items.en: It is the small repeated actions that count the most.
items.note: ce sont ... qui highlights the subject

activities.title: Habitudes et tendances
activities.intro: Practise trend expressions and infinitives used as subjects.

questions.prompt: Which French phrase means 'less and less'?
questions.explanation: De moins en moins marks a falling trend; de temps en temps means from time to time.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses an infinitive as the subject correctly?
questions.explanation: An infinitive subject counts as singular, so the verb is est and the adjective stays singular.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Je ___ le chauffage d'un degré chaque soir.' (meaning: I turn down)
questions.explanation: Baisser is a regular -er verb, so the je form ends in -e.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Je vais au travail à pied ___ six mois.' (for the last six months, still true)
questions.explanation: Depuis with the present tense describes something that started in the past and continues now.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'It is the small repeated actions that count the most.'
questions.explanation: Ce sont introduces a plural highlighted subject, and qui carries the verb that follows.

activities.title: Chez nous, on a changé nos habitudes
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague doubts that small habits matter, and you answer with trend expressions and infinitive subjects.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Franchement, trier ses déchets, ça change quoi ?
lines.en: Honestly, does sorting your waste change anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trier ses déchets prend cinq minutes et ça évite beaucoup de gaspillage.
lines.en: Sorting your waste takes five minutes and it prevents a lot of waste.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as changé d'autres choses chez toi ?
lines.en: Have you changed other things at home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, nous consommons de moins en moins d'eau chaude.
lines.en: Yes, we use less and less hot water.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour les transports ?
lines.en: And what about transport?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je me déplace à vélo depuis un an, même quand il pleut.
lines.en: I have been getting around by bike for a year, even when it rains.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu n'as pas l'impression que c'est trop peu ?
lines.en: Do you not feel that it is too little?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, ce sont les petits gestes répétés qui comptent le plus.
lines.en: No, it is the small repeated actions that count the most.

days.title: La presse et l'information : distinguer les sources
days.objective: You can recognise when written news is presenting unconfirmed information and say how you check a source before sharing it.
days.paretoFocus: The journalistic conditional is everywhere in written news, and mistaking it for a fact is the single most common misreading of French headlines.
drills: Rewrite two confirmed facts as unconfirmed news using the conditional.
Say aloud three questions you ask yourself before sharing an article.
Find two sentences in any French text and decide whether they state a fact or a rumour.

items: What does the conditional signal in a news report?
Give the French phrase meaning 'apparently, but nothing is confirmed'.
Which preposition follows avant when the next word is a verb?

activities.title: Vérifier une information
activities.intro: In written news, the conditional marks information that is not confirmed yet, so aurait fait means reportedly caused. Il paraît que introduces hearsay. Avant de plus an infinitive says what you do first.

items.hr: D'après ce journal, l'accident aurait fait deux blessés.
items.en: According to this newspaper, the accident reportedly injured two people.

items.hr: Il paraît que le maire va démissionner, mais rien n'est confirmé.
items.en: Apparently the mayor is going to resign, but nothing is confirmed.

items.hr: Avant de partager un article, je regarde qui l'a écrit.
items.en: Before sharing an article, I look at who wrote it.

items.hr: Ce site ne cite jamais ses sources.
items.en: This site never quotes its sources.
items.note: citer ses sources = to quote your sources

items.hr: Une information reprise partout n'est pas forcément vraie.
items.en: Information repeated everywhere is not necessarily true.

activities.title: Fait confirmé ou rumeur ?
activities.intro: Practise the journalistic conditional and the language of checking information.

questions.prompt: In a news report, 'Le voleur aurait pris la fuite' means:
questions.explanation: The past conditional in the press signals that the fact is reported but not yet confirmed.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct completion: 'Avant ___ partager un article, lisez-le en entier.'
questions.explanation: Avant needs de before an infinitive; avant que would be followed by a subject and a subjunctive verb.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Il ___ que le concert est annulé, mais je n'ai aucune confirmation.' (apparently)
questions.explanation: The impersonal expression il paraît que introduces something you heard but cannot confirm.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Ce blog ne cite jamais ses ___.' (where the information comes from)
questions.explanation: Citer ses sources is the fixed expression for naming where information comes from, and it is used in the plural.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Information repeated everywhere is not necessarily true.'
questions.explanation: The past participle reprise agrees with the feminine noun, and forcément sits between pas and the adjective.

activities.title: Cette info est-elle fiable ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend has read a surprising story online and you use the conditional and source checking language to react.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as vu ? L'usine du quartier va fermer !
lines.en: Have you seen? The factory in the neighbourhood is going to close!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Attends, c'est confirmé ou il paraît seulement que c'est prévu ?
lines.en: Wait, is it confirmed or is it only said to be planned?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est écrit noir sur blanc dans un article.
lines.en: It is written clearly in an article.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'article dit peut-être que la direction aurait annoncé la fermeture, ce n'est pas pareil.
lines.en: The article maybe says that the management reportedly announced the closure, which is not the same thing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu crois que c'est faux ?
lines.en: Do you think it is false?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne sais pas, mais ce site ne cite jamais ses sources.
lines.en: I do not know, but this site never quotes its sources.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourtant tout le monde le partage.
lines.en: Still, everyone is sharing it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Une information reprise partout n'est pas forcément vraie : avant de partager, je vérifie.
lines.en: Information repeated everywhere is not necessarily true: before sharing, I check.

days.title: Le sport et la compétition : commenter un événement
days.objective: You can comment on a live sports event, give the score and react with exclamations.
days.paretoFocus: Venir de and être en train de let you place an action just before or right around now, which is the core of any live commentary or eyewitness account.
drills: Describe three actions happening right now using être en train de.
Say three things that have just happened using venir de.
Give two scores aloud in French and add an exclamation with quel or quelle.

items: How do you say 'they have just scored' in French?
Give the French for 'two goals to nil'.
Which word introduces an exclamation before a masculine noun?

activities.title: Commenter un match en direct
activities.intro: Venir de plus an infinitive describes an action that has just happened. Être en train de plus an infinitive describes an action in progress. Quel and quelle open exclamations and agree with the noun.

items.hr: L'équipe locale mène deux buts à zéro à la mi-temps.
items.en: The local team leads two goals to nil at half time.
items.note: mener = to lead; scores use à

items.hr: Ils sont en train de perdre le contrôle du match.
items.en: They are losing control of the match.

items.hr: Le gardien vient d'arrêter un tir difficile.
items.en: The goalkeeper has just saved a difficult shot.
items.note: venir de + infinitive = to have just done

items.hr: Quelle fin de match incroyable !
items.en: What an incredible end to the match!
items.note: quelle agrees with the feminine noun fin

items.hr: Elle a battu son record personnel de deux secondes.
items.en: She beat her personal record by two seconds.

activities.title: En direct du stade
activities.intro: Practise recent past, ongoing action and exclamations.

questions.prompt: 'Le gardien vient d'arrêter un tir' means:
options: The goalkeeper is going to save a shot.
The goalkeeper has just saved a shot.
The goalkeeper used to save shots.
The goalkeeper wants to save a shot.
questions.answer: The goalkeeper has just saved a shot.
questions.explanation: Venir conjugated in the present plus de plus an infinitive expresses the immediate past.

questions.prompt: Which exclamation is correct?
questions.explanation: Match is masculine, so the exclamative word is quel and no article is used.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Ils sont en ___ de gagner le match.' (in the process of)
questions.explanation: The fixed expression être en train de plus an infinitive marks an action in progress.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: 'Elle a ___ son record personnel hier soir.' (past participle of battre)
questions.explanation: Battre is irregular and its past participle is battu, used here with avoir.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The local team leads two goals to nil at half time.'
questions.explanation: The adjective follows the noun, then the verb, then the score with à, then the time expression.

activities.title: Devant le match
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are watching a match with a friend who arrived late, and you comment using venir de and être en train de.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'arrive en retard, où en est le match ?
lines.en: I am arriving late, where is the match at?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'équipe locale mène deux buts à zéro.
lines.en: The local team leads two goals to nil.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sérieux ? Il s'est passé quoi ?
lines.en: Seriously? What happened?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le numéro dix vient de marquer un but magnifique.
lines.en: Number ten has just scored a wonderful goal.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et les autres, ils jouent comment ?
lines.en: And the others, how are they playing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mal, ils sont en train de perdre le contrôle du milieu de terrain.
lines.en: Badly, they are losing control of the midfield.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Regarde, le gardien sort !
lines.en: Look, the goalkeeper is coming out!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quelle fin de match incroyable !
lines.en: What an incredible end to the match!

days.title: La chanson : comprendre des paroles simples
days.objective: You can decode the shortened forms used in sung and casual French and follow the structure of a song.
days.paretoFocus: Songs drop the ne of the negation and squeeze pronouns together, exactly like real conversation, so learning to hear those forms unlocks both lyrics and everyday speech.
drills: Say five negative sentences aloud without the ne, the way they are sung.
Write the full written form of three shortened phrases you know.
Listen to any French song once and write down the words of the chorus you recognise.
