questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'She earns as much money as her brother.'
questions.explanation: Autant d'argent que expresses an equal quantity of money.

activities.title: La vie change
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Chat about how life is changing, using advanced comparisons.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu trouves que la vie est plus difficile qu'avant ?
lines.en: Do you find life harder than before?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, tout devient de plus en plus cher.
lines.en: Yes, everything is getting more and more expensive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est vrai, je dépense autant d'argent qu'avant mais j'ai moins.
lines.en: True, I spend as much money as before but I have less.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Et je travaille autant que toi, sans plus de repos.
lines.en: And I work as much as you, without more rest.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Au moins, on garde le même bon moral qu'avant !
lines.en: At least we keep the same good spirits as before!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Exactement, et on rit de plus en plus souvent.
lines.en: Exactly, and we laugh more and more often.

days.title: Les verbes à double construction : demander à quelqu'un de
days.objective: Build sentences with verbs that take à plus a person and de plus an infinitive, such as demander, permettre and interdire.
days.paretoFocus: These verbs are the backbone of reporting requests and rules, and mixing up à and de is one of the most persistent B1 errors.
drills: Write three sentences using demander, conseiller or promettre with à quelqu'un de plus an infinitive.
Replace the person after à with an indirect pronoun (lui, leur) in two sentences.
Turn three direct orders into reported requests using demander à... de.

items: Which preposition comes before the person after demander?
Which preposition comes before the infinitive after permettre?
How do you replace à mon frère in demander à mon frère de partir?

activities.title: À la personne, de + infinitif
activities.intro: Some verbs point to a person with à and to an action with de: demander à quelqu'un de faire, permettre à quelqu'un de faire, conseiller, interdire, promettre. The person introduced by à becomes an indirect pronoun, lui or leur.

items.hr: J'ai demandé à mon voisin de baisser le son.
items.en: I asked my neighbour to turn down the volume.

items.hr: Mes parents me permettent de sortir le soir.
items.en: My parents allow me to go out in the evening.
items.note: permettre à quelqu'un de; à moi becomes me

items.hr: Le médecin lui a conseillé de se reposer.
items.en: The doctor advised him to rest.
items.note: conseiller à quelqu'un de; lui = à lui

items.hr: On interdit aux voitures de circuler ici.
items.en: Cars are forbidden to drive here.

items.hr: Elle a promis à ses amis de revenir vite.
items.en: She promised her friends to come back soon.

activities.title: À ou de ?
activities.intro: Choose the right preposition or pronoun for these double-construction verbs.

questions.prompt: J'ai demandé ___ mon frère ___ m'aider.
questions.explanation: Demander takes à before the person and de before the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Rappel du pronom : Le professeur ___ a permis de partir plus tôt.
questions.explanation: À plus a person becomes the indirect pronoun lui.

questions.prompt: Mes parents m'interdisent ___ rentrer tard. (préposition)
questions.explanation: Interdire à quelqu'un de plus infinitive, so the infinitive follows de.

questions.prompt: Elle a conseillé ___ son ami de consulter un médecin. (préposition)
questions.explanation: Conseiller takes à before the person.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I asked my brother to help me.'
questions.explanation: Demander à mon frère de m'aider follows the à-person, de-infinitive pattern.

activities.title: Les règles à la maison
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Compare house rules using double-construction verbs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tes parents te permettent de sortir le week-end ?
lines.en: Do your parents let you go out at the weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, mais ils me demandent de rentrer avant minuit.
lines.en: Yes, but they ask me to be home before midnight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Les miens m'interdisent de sortir en semaine.
lines.en: Mine forbid me to go out on weekdays.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu leur as demandé de changer un peu les règles ?
lines.en: Have you asked them to change the rules a bit?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, et ma mère m'a promis d'y réfléchir.
lines.en: Yes, and my mother promised me to think about it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je te conseille de leur parler calmement.
lines.en: I advise you to talk to them calmly.

days.title: Écoute : comprendre les annonces courtes
days.objective: Catch the essential detail, time, place, price or instruction, in short public announcements.
days.paretoFocus: Short announcements in stations, shops and phone menus carry one key fact each, and training your ear to grab just that fact saves you in every listening test.
drills: Before an announcement, decide in advance which single detail you need, then listen only for it.
Listen for numbers and times first, since they carry most announcements.
Say back one heard announcement in your own words, keeping only the key information.

items: What should you read or decide before an announcement starts?
Which kinds of words carry most of the meaning in an announcement?
What do you do if you miss one word?

activities.title: Repérer l'information clé
activities.intro: In a short-announcement task you hear a message once or twice; the trick is to know what the question asks before you listen, then track only that detail. Numbers, times and places are the usual targets, and a single word matters less than the fact behind it.

items.hr: Le train à destination de Lyon partira du quai numéro quatre à quinze heures dix.
items.en: The train to Lyon will leave from platform four at ten past three.
items.note: listen for the platform number and the time

items.hr: En raison de travaux, le magasin fermera à dix-huit heures ce soir.
items.en: Due to works, the shop will close at six p.m. tonight.
items.note: catch the closing time and the reason

items.hr: Les voyageurs sont priés de ne pas laisser leurs bagages sans surveillance.
items.en: Passengers are asked not to leave their luggage unattended.

items.hr: La réunion de demain est reportée à jeudi prochain, même salle.
items.en: Tomorrow's meeting is postponed to next Thursday, same room.
items.note: the change of date is the key information

items.hr: Pour toute information, appuyez sur la touche étoile.
items.en: For any information, press the star key.

activities.title: Attraper l'essentiel
activities.intro: Apply the strategy and answer questions on the announcements above.

questions.prompt: Vous devez noter l'heure d'un train. Avant d'écouter, il vaut mieux :
questions.explanation: Knowing what to listen for lets you focus on the one detail that matters.

questions.prompt: « Le magasin fermera à dix-huit heures. » À quelle heure ferme le magasin ?
questions.explanation: Dix-huit heures is six p.m., written 18 h.

questions.prompt: Dans l'annonce, le train part du quai numéro ___.
questions.explanation: The announcement names platform four, quai numéro quatre.

questions.prompt: « La réunion est reportée à jeudi. » Le nouveau jour est ___.
questions.explanation: The key detail of the change is the new day, jeudi.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The train will leave from platform two.'
questions.explanation: Partira du quai numéro deux states the departure platform.

activities.title: À la gare
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. React to station announcements with a friend, focusing on the key fact.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as entendu l'annonce ? Quel quai pour Lyon ?
lines.en: Did you hear the announcement? Which platform for Lyon?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le quai numéro quatre, à quinze heures dix.
lines.en: Platform four, at ten past three.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah bon ? Je croyais que c'était le quai deux.
lines.en: Really? I thought it was platform two.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, écoute bien : ils répètent toujours le numéro.
lines.en: No, listen carefully: they always repeat the number.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as raison, il faut se concentrer sur l'essentiel.
lines.en: You're right, we must focus on the essentials.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Exactement, on note l'heure et le quai, c'est tout.
lines.en: Exactly, we note the time and the platform, that's all.

days.title: Écoute : saisir l'idée principale d'un reportage
days.objective: Identify the main idea and the speaker's attitude in a short radio-style report.
days.paretoFocus: Longer recordings test whether you grasp the gist, not every word, and listening for the topic sentence and the conclusion is the skill that unlocks them.
drills: Guess the vocabulary a report will use from its topic before you listen.
After a report, sum up its main idea in one sentence.
Listen for a contrast word to catch where the speaker changes direction.

items: Which two sentences of a report usually carry the main idea?
How can you prepare vocabulary before a report begins?
Which word signals that the speaker is about to give an opposite view?

activities.title: L'idée générale d'abord
activities.intro: A report asks for the main idea, not every detail. Note the topic in the first sentence, then follow whether the speaker is positive or negative. Contrast words such as pourtant or cependant signal a change of view, and en résumé announces the conclusion.

items.hr: Aujourd'hui, nous parlons du succès des vélos en libre-service dans les villes.
items.en: Today we're talking about the success of self-service bikes in cities.
items.note: the first sentence announces the topic

items.hr: De plus en plus d'habitants les utilisent pour aller au travail.
items.en: More and more residents use them to get to work.

items.hr: Selon une étude, l'air des villes est devenu un peu plus propre.
items.en: According to a study, city air has become a little cleaner.
items.note: a figure or source is a detail to note

items.hr: Certains regrettent pourtant le manque de pistes cyclables.
items.en: Some, however, regret the lack of bike lanes.

items.hr: En résumé, le bilan reste très positif pour la plupart des usagers.
items.en: In short, the outcome stays very positive for most users.

activities.title: Trouver le message
activities.intro: Work on the gist and on how the reporter feels about the topic in the clip above.

questions.prompt: Pour trouver l'idée principale d'un reportage, il faut surtout écouter :
options: la première et la dernière phrase
questions.answer: la première et la dernière phrase
questions.explanation: The topic sentence and the conclusion usually carry the main idea.

questions.prompt: Quel est le sujet du reportage ?
questions.explanation: The first sentence names the topic: self-service bikes.

questions.prompt: Le mot « ___ » annonce une opinion contraire dans le reportage.
questions.explanation: Pourtant signals a shift to an opposing view.

questions.prompt: Rappel du connecteur : l'expression « en ___ » introduit la conclusion.
questions.explanation: En résumé introduces the closing summary.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'More and more people use the bus.'
questions.explanation: De plus en plus de gens expresses a rising number of people.

activities.title: On en a compris quoi ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Discuss what you understood from a radio piece.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as compris de quoi parlait le reportage ?
lines.en: Did you understand what the report was about?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, du succès des vélos partagés en ville.
lines.en: Yes, about the success of shared bikes in the city.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et l'avis du journaliste était plutôt positif ?
lines.en: And was the journalist's opinion rather positive?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Globalement oui, malgré le manque de pistes cyclables.
lines.en: Overall yes, despite the lack of bike lanes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment tu fais pour retenir l'essentiel ?
lines.en: How do you manage to remember the main point?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'écoute la première phrase et la conclusion, surtout.
lines.en: I listen to the first sentence and the conclusion, mostly.

days.title: Écoute : suivre qui dit quoi dans une conversation
days.objective: Follow a dialogue between two people and tell which speaker holds which opinion.
days.paretoFocus: Conversation tasks turn on tracking two voices, and marking each speaker's stance keeps you from confusing who said what under time pressure.
drills: While listening to two speakers, mark each opinion with a plus or a minus.
Listen for phrases of agreement and disagreement to track the two positions.
Say which speaker was more positive after hearing a short exchange.

items: How can you quickly note two speakers' opposite opinions?
Which phrases show that a speaker disagrees?
What does the question usually ask about in a conversation task?

activities.title: Deux voix, deux avis
activities.intro: In a conversation two voices alternate and the task often asks who thinks what. Note each speaker's position with a plus or a minus, and listen for markers of agreement (je suis d'accord) and disagreement (je ne suis pas d'accord). Watch for a speaker who softens or nuances a view.

items.hr: Journaliste : Pensez-vous que le télétravail soit une bonne chose ?
items.en: Journalist: Do you think remote work is a good thing?

items.hr: La femme : Pour moi, c'est un vrai avantage, je gagne du temps.
items.en: The woman: For me, it's a real advantage, I save time.

items.hr: L'homme : Je ne suis pas d'accord, on se sent vite isolé.
items.en: The man: I don't agree, you quickly feel isolated.

items.hr: La femme : Cependant, il faut de la discipline, je l'admets.
items.en: The woman: However, you need discipline, I admit it.

items.hr: L'homme : Sur ce point, nous sommes d'accord.
items.en: The man: On that point, we agree.

activities.title: Qui pense quoi ?
activities.intro: Track the two speakers' positions in the interview above.

questions.prompt: Pour suivre une conversation, il est utile de :
questions.explanation: Marking each speaker's stance keeps the two positions clear.

questions.prompt: Qui pense que le télétravail isole les gens ?
questions.explanation: The man disagrees and says you feel isolated.

questions.prompt: La femme trouve que le télétravail est un ___. (mot positif de l'extrait)
questions.explanation: She calls it un vrai avantage, a real advantage.

questions.prompt: Rappel de l'opinion : L'homme dit « Je ne pense pas que ce ___ une bonne idée. » (être)
questions.explanation: Ne pas penser que triggers the subjunctive, so être becomes soit.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'On this point, I agree with you.'
questions.explanation: Je suis d'accord avec toi expresses agreement with the other speaker.

activities.title: Ils étaient d'accord ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Report who held which view in an interview you heard.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dans l'interview, les deux invités étaient d'accord ?
lines.en: In the interview, did the two guests agree?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas vraiment, la femme était pour, l'homme contre.
lines.en: Not really, the woman was for, the man against.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment tu as retenu qui disait quoi ?
lines.en: How did you remember who said what?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai mis un plus et un moins à côté de chaque voix.
lines.en: I put a plus and a minus next to each voice.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ils étaient d'accord sur quelque chose ?
lines.en: And did they agree on anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, tous les deux pensent qu'il faut de la discipline.
lines.en: Yes, both think you need discipline.

days.title: Écoute : gérer les trois exercices
days.objective: Manage a full listening section by using the pauses to read questions and the two hearings for different goals.
days.paretoFocus: Knowing how to use the seconds before a recording and the gap between two hearings turns a stressful listening section into a routine you control.
drills: Use the seconds before a recording to read every question first.
On a first hearing aim for the gist, on a second aim for the missing details.
Practise moving on calmly when one word escapes you.

items: What is the goal of the first hearing versus the second?
What should you do in the seconds before a recording?
What is the rule when a word escapes you?

activities.title: Une méthode pour l'écoute
activities.intro: A listening section has several recordings, each played once or twice. Use the seconds before each one to read the questions, the first hearing for the gist, and the second for the details you missed. Never freeze on one word, and use the final seconds to check your answers.

items.hr: Avant l'écoute, lisez les questions pour savoir quoi chercher.
items.en: Before listening, read the questions to know what to look for.

items.hr: À la première écoute, cherchez l'idée générale.
items.en: On the first listening, look for the general idea.

items.hr: À la deuxième écoute, notez les détails précis.
items.en: On the second listening, note the precise details.

items.hr: Si un mot vous échappe, continuez sans paniquer.
items.en: If a word escapes you, keep going without panicking.

items.hr: Vérifiez vos réponses pendant les dernières secondes.
items.en: Check your answers during the last few seconds.

activities.title: La bonne méthode
activities.intro: Confirm the strategy for handling the listening section.

questions.prompt: À la deuxième écoute, votre objectif principal est :
questions.explanation: The second hearing is for the details you missed first time.

questions.prompt: Que faire si vous ne comprenez pas un mot ?
questions.explanation: You keep going; one missed word rarely costs the answer.

questions.prompt: Avant chaque enregistrement, il faut ___ les questions.
questions.explanation: Reading the questions first tells you what to listen for.

questions.prompt: Rappel de l'impératif : Conseil à un ami : « ___ les questions avant d'écouter ! » (lire, tu)
questions.explanation: The tu imperative of lire is Lis.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Read the questions before listening.'
questions.explanation: Lisez les questions avant d'écouter states the first step.

activities.title: Avant l'épreuve
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Coach a nervous friend before the listening section.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai peur de l'épreuve d'écoute, tu as des conseils ?
lines.en: I'm scared of the listening test, do you have any tips?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, lis toujours les questions avant chaque enregistrement.
lines.en: Yes, always read the questions before each recording.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et si je rate un mot important ?
lines.en: And if I miss an important word?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Continue, ne bloque pas, l'idée générale suffit d'abord.
lines.en: Keep going, don't freeze, the general idea is enough first.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la deuxième écoute, elle sert à quoi ?
lines.en: And what's the second listening for?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À noter les détails que tu as manqués la première fois.
lines.en: To note the details you missed the first time.

days.title: Lecture : repérer l'information dans un texte
days.objective: Locate specific facts in an informative text by scanning titles, paragraphs and key words.
days.paretoFocus: Reading tasks reward finding facts fast, not reading every word, and scanning by keyword is a skill you reuse for timetables, notices and articles alike.
drills: Read only the title first and predict the topic before reading on.
Underline the key word in a question, then hunt for it in the text.
Find three facts, a date, a number and a condition, without reading the whole text.

items: What do you read first to predict a text's topic?
How does a key word in the question help you?
Do you need to read every word to find a specific fact?

activities.title: Lire pour trouver
activities.intro: Reading tasks rarely need every word. Use the title to predict the topic, then scan each paragraph for the fact the question asks. A key word in the question, a name, a number or a date, leads you straight to the right line.

items.hr: La nouvelle médiathèque ouvrira ses portes le premier septembre.
items.en: The new media library will open on the first of September.

items.hr: Elle proposera plus de trente mille livres et de nombreux films.
items.en: It will offer more than thirty thousand books and many films.

items.hr: L'inscription est gratuite pour les moins de dix-huit ans.
items.en: Registration is free for under-eighteens.

items.hr: Des ateliers de lecture auront lieu chaque mercredi après-midi.
items.en: Reading workshops will take place every Wednesday afternoon.

items.hr: Le bâtiment est accessible aux personnes à mobilité réduite.
items.en: The building is accessible to people with reduced mobility.

activities.title: Chercher un fait
activities.intro: Scan the text above to answer each question.

questions.prompt: Pour trouver une information précise dans un texte, on utilise :
questions.explanation: Scanning titles and key words finds a specific fact quickly.

questions.prompt: L'inscription est gratuite pour qui ?
questions.explanation: The text states registration is free for under-eighteens.

questions.prompt: La médiathèque ouvrira le premier ___.
questions.explanation: The opening date given is the first of September.

questions.prompt: Elle proposera plus de trente mille ___.
questions.explanation: The counted item is books, livres.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The library opens on Monday.'
questions.explanation: La bibliothèque ouvre le lundi states the opening day.

activities.title: On y va quand ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Plan a visit using facts scanned from an article.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu sais quand la nouvelle médiathèque ouvre ?
lines.en: Do you know when the new media library opens?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, le premier septembre, c'est écrit dans l'article.
lines.en: Yes, on the first of September, it's written in the article.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et c'est payant pour s'inscrire ?
lines.en: And do you have to pay to register?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, c'est gratuit pour les moins de dix-huit ans.
lines.en: No, it's free for under-eighteens.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment tu trouves ces infos si vite ?
lines.en: How do you find this info so fast?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je cherche les chiffres et les mots-clés dans chaque paragraphe.
lines.en: I look for numbers and key words in each paragraph.

days.title: Lecture : dégager la position de l'auteur
days.objective: Identify the author's opinion and separate facts from personal viewpoints in a text.
days.paretoFocus: Many texts argue rather than inform, and telling the author's opinion from the facts is what the harder reading questions really test.
drills: Underline every value word (heureusement, malheureusement) in a short opinion text.
Sort three sentences from a text into fact and opinion.
State the author's overall position in one sentence.

items: Which kind of word signals the author's opinion rather than a fact?
How do you tell a fact from an opinion in a text?
Which connector introduces an opposing view?

activities.title: Fait ou opinion
activities.intro: Some texts give an opinion, not just facts. Look for value words such as heureusement or malheureusement, and phrases like selon moi or il est clair que. Separate what is a fact from what the author thinks about it, and find the sentence where the author concludes.

items.hr: Selon l'auteur, les téléphones n'ont pas leur place en classe.
items.en: According to the author, phones have no place in class.

items.hr: Il est vrai que ces appareils distraient beaucoup les élèves.
items.en: It is true that these devices distract pupils a lot.
items.note: il est vrai que introduces an accepted fact

items.hr: Malheureusement, les résultats baissent, affirme-t-il.
items.en: Unfortunately, results are dropping, he claims.

items.hr: Cependant, certains professeurs les trouvent utiles.
items.en: However, some teachers find them useful.

items.hr: L'auteur conclut qu'il faudrait mieux les encadrer.
items.en: The author concludes that they should be better regulated.

activities.title: Quelle est sa position ?
activities.intro: Separate fact from opinion and find the author's stance.

questions.prompt: Le mot « malheureusement » montre :
questions.explanation: A value word like malheureusement signals the author's judgement.

questions.prompt: Quelle est la position de l'auteur sur les téléphones en classe ?
questions.explanation: He says phones have no place in class, so he is against.

questions.prompt: L'expression « selon l'___ » introduit une opinion.
questions.explanation: Selon l'auteur signals the writer's viewpoint.

questions.prompt: Le connecteur qui introduit une idée contraire ici est « ___ ».
questions.explanation: Cependant introduces the opposing view.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'According to the author, this idea is wrong.'
questions.explanation: Selon l'auteur frames the following statement as the writer's opinion.

activities.title: On en pense quoi ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Discuss an opinion article and how you spotted the author's stance.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as lu l'article sur les téléphones à l'école ?
lines.en: Did you read the article about phones at school?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, l'auteur est clairement contre leur usage en classe.
lines.en: Yes, the author is clearly against their use in class.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment tu sais que c'est son opinion et pas un fait ?
lines.en: How do you know it's his opinion and not a fact?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il utilise « malheureusement », c'est un mot de jugement.
lines.en: He uses 'unfortunately', it's a judgement word.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et il reconnaît quand même un avis contraire ?
lines.en: And does he still acknowledge an opposing view?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, avec « cependant », il cite les professeurs favorables.
lines.en: Yes, with 'however', he mentions the teachers in favour.

days.title: Écrit : l'essai d'opinion, le plan et les idées
days.objective: Plan a short opinion essay with a clear introduction, two arguments each with an example, and a conclusion.
days.paretoFocus: A short opinion essay lives or dies on its plan, and the intro-arguments-conclusion shape is a template you can fill for almost any prompt.
drills: Draft a four-part plan for one opinion topic: intro, two arguments, conclusion.
Add one concrete example under each argument.
Budget roughly forty words per part to hit the length.

items: What are the four parts of a short opinion essay?
What should follow each argument to make it convincing?
How do you plan the length before writing?

activities.title: Bâtir un essai
activities.intro: A short written opinion needs a plan before you write. State your opinion in the introduction, give two arguments, each supported by an example, and restate your view in the conclusion. Aim for the required length by planning roughly forty words per part.

items.hr: À mon avis, il faut encourager les transports en commun.
items.en: In my opinion, we should encourage public transport.

items.hr: D'abord, ils réduisent la pollution dans les grandes villes.
items.en: First, they reduce pollution in big cities.

items.hr: Par exemple, l'air de ma ville est meilleur depuis le nouveau tramway.
items.en: For example, the air in my town is better since the new tram.

items.hr: Ensuite, ils coûtent moins cher que la voiture.
items.en: Then, they cost less than the car.

items.hr: Pour conclure, je pense que c'est une solution d'avenir.
items.en: To conclude, I think it's a solution for the future.

activities.title: Structurer ses idées
activities.intro: Practise the structure and connectors of an opinion essay.

questions.prompt: Dans un essai d'opinion, l'introduction sert à :
questions.explanation: The introduction states your position on the topic.

questions.prompt: Quel mot introduit un exemple ?
questions.explanation: Par exemple introduces an illustration of an argument.

questions.prompt: On termine un essai par une phrase de ___.
questions.explanation: The final part is the conclusion, restating your view.

questions.prompt: Rappel des connecteurs : on présente le premier argument avec « d'abord » et le second avec « ___ ».
questions.explanation: Ensuite links to the second argument in a sequence.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'In my opinion, we must protect nature.'
questions.explanation: À mon avis opens an opinion, followed by il faut plus the infinitive.

activities.title: Par où commencer ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Help a friend outline an opinion text.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je dois écrire un texte d'opinion, par où je commence ?
lines.en: I have to write an opinion text, where do I start?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Par un plan : ton avis, deux arguments, une conclusion.
lines.en: With a plan: your opinion, two arguments, a conclusion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et je mets des exemples ?
lines.en: And do I add examples?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, un exemple concret après chaque argument, c'est mieux.
lines.en: Yes, a concrete example after each argument is better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment je respecte le nombre de mots ?
lines.en: How do I respect the word count?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Compte environ quarante mots par partie, ça suffit.
lines.en: Count about forty words per part, that's enough.

days.title: Écrit : l'essai d'opinion, connecteurs et relecture
days.objective: Link ideas with connectors and self-correct a draft by checking verbs, agreement and spelling.
days.paretoFocus: Ideas alone do not score; linking them clearly and catching your own verb and agreement errors on a final read is what lifts a written mark.
drills: Rewrite a plain paragraph adding one connector between each sentence.
Do a final read of a draft looking only at verb endings and agreements.
Replace one weak sentence like 'c'est bien' with a more precise one.

items: Which connector marks an opposition?
What should you check first during a final re-read?
How do you improve a sentence that is too vague?

activities.title: Relier et relire
activities.intro: A good short essay is not just ideas, it is linked ideas. Connectors show the reader your logic. A final re-read catches the errors that cost points, above all verb endings and agreements, and lets you replace a weak sentence with a sharper one.

items.hr: De plus, cette mesure aiderait les familles modestes.
items.en: Moreover, this measure would help low-income families.

items.hr: En revanche, elle coûterait cher à l'État.
items.en: On the other hand, it would be expensive for the State.

items.hr: C'est pourquoi je reste favorable à cette idée.
items.en: That's why I remain in favour of this idea.

items.hr: Avant de rendre, je relis pour vérifier les accords.
items.en: Before handing in, I re-read to check the agreements.

items.hr: Je remplace « je pense que c'est bien » par une phrase plus précise.
items.en: I replace 'I think it's good' with a more precise sentence.
items.note: improve a sentence that is too simple

activities.title: Corriger et améliorer
activities.intro: Work on connectors and on self-correction.

questions.prompt: Quel connecteur marque l'opposition ?
questions.explanation: En revanche introduces a contrasting point.

questions.prompt: Pendant la relecture, il faut surtout vérifier :
questions.explanation: Agreements and verb endings are where most points are lost.

questions.prompt: Le connecteur « c'est ___ » introduit une conséquence.
questions.explanation: C'est pourquoi introduces the consequence that follows.

questions.prompt: Rappel de l'accord : corrigez « Les mesures que j'ai propos___ sont utiles. »
questions.explanation: The direct object les mesures comes before avoir, so the participle agrees: proposées.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Moreover, this idea would help everyone.'
questions.explanation: De plus adds an argument, and aiderait is the conditionnel of aider.

activities.title: Ma rédaction est plate
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Help a friend revise a flat draft.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai fini mon essai, mais il semble un peu plat.
lines.en: I finished my essay, but it seems a bit flat.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ajoute des connecteurs comme « de plus » et « en revanche ».
lines.en: Add connectors like 'moreover' and 'on the other hand'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord. Et pour les fautes ?
lines.en: Okay. And for the mistakes?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Relis à la fin et vérifie tous les accords.
lines.en: Re-read at the end and check all the agreements.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'écris souvent « je pense que c'est bien », c'est faible ?
lines.en: I often write 'I think it's good', is that weak?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, remplace-le par une phrase plus précise.
lines.en: Yes, replace it with a more precise sentence.

days.title: Écrit : la lettre au courrier des lecteurs
days.objective: Write a reader's letter that reacts to an article with a clear reason, an opinion and a suitable closing.
days.paretoFocus: Reacting to something you have read is a common writing task, and the react-give-reason-close shape keeps your letter focused and engaged.
drills: Write the opening line of a reader's letter naming the article you react to.
Give your opinion and back it with one reason from your own experience.
Close the letter politely, then check the register is respectful, not stiff.

items: What is a reader's letter for?
Which phrase introduces a softened opinion?
How does a reader's letter close differ from a strictly official one?

activities.title: Réagir par écrit
activities.intro: A reader's letter reacts to something you have read. Greet the paper or readers, say what you are reacting to, give your opinion with one reason, and close politely. The tone is engaged but respectful, a little lighter than a strictly official letter.

items.hr: J'ai lu avec intérêt votre article sur le gaspillage alimentaire.
items.en: I read your article on food waste with interest.
items.note: say what you are reacting to

items.hr: Je souhaite réagir à ce sujet qui me tient à cœur.
items.en: I wish to react to this topic which is close to my heart.

items.hr: En effet, dans mon quartier, beaucoup de nourriture est jetée.
items.en: Indeed, in my neighbourhood, a lot of food is thrown away.
items.note: en effet supports the opinion with a fact

items.hr: Il me semble qu'une solution simple existe pourtant.
items.en: It seems to me, however, that a simple solution does exist.
items.note: il me semble que = softened opinion

items.hr: Je vous remercie de votre attention et vous salue cordialement.
items.en: I thank you for your attention and send my cordial regards.
items.note: a closing lighter than an official letter

activities.title: Une lettre engagée
activities.intro: Work on the moves and register of a reader's letter.

questions.prompt: Une lettre au courrier des lecteurs sert à :
questions.explanation: Its purpose is to react to something published.

questions.prompt: Rappel du registre formel : pour une lettre plus officielle, on termine plutôt par :
options: je vous prie d'agréer mes salutations distinguées
questions.answer: je vous prie d'agréer mes salutations distinguées
questions.explanation: The strictly official closing is je vous prie d'agréer mes salutations distinguées.

questions.prompt: « J'ai lu avec ___ votre article. » (nom qui montre l'attention)
questions.explanation: Avec intérêt shows engaged attention.

questions.prompt: Pour donner un avis nuancé, on écrit « il me ___ que ». (verbe sembler)
questions.explanation: Il me semble que softens an opinion.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I wish to react to this article.'
questions.explanation: Je souhaite réagir à cet article announces your intention.

activities.title: Un article m'a énervé
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Coach a friend who wants to answer a newspaper article in writing, keeping it short and polite.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je veux répondre à un article qui m'a énervé.
lines.en: I want to respond to an article that annoyed me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Écris une lettre au courrier des lecteurs, alors.
lines.en: Write a reader's letter, then.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je commence comment ?
lines.en: How do I start?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dis d'abord quel article tu as lu et pourquoi tu réagis.
lines.en: First say which article you read and why you're reacting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et je peux rester poli même si je ne suis pas d'accord ?
lines.en: And can I stay polite even if I disagree?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr, un ton respectueux est toujours plus convaincant.
lines.en: Of course, a respectful tone is always more convincing.

days.title: Oral : l'entretien dirigé, se présenter
days.objective: Introduce yourself in a guided interview and expand short questions into full, developed answers.
days.paretoFocus: The opening interview sets the tone of a speaking test, and the habit of never answering in one word is what makes you sound fluent from the first minute.
drills: Answer three simple questions about yourself, adding a reason or an example to each.
Prepare a two-sentence self-introduction with one memorable detail.
Steer a question toward a topic you can talk about at length.

items: What must you never do when answering an interview question?
What can you add to expand a short answer?
How do you introduce yourself with a memorable detail?

activities.title: Développer ses réponses
activities.intro: The interview opens the speaking test. The examiner asks simple questions about you; the skill is never to answer in one word. Add a reason, an example or a plan to every answer, and steer toward topics you can talk about. A small personal detail makes your introduction memorable.

items.hr: Je m'appelle Sofia et je viens d'une petite ville du sud.
items.en: My name is Sofia and I come from a small town in the south.

items.hr: J'apprends le français parce que je veux travailler à l'étranger.
items.en: I'm learning French because I want to work abroad.
items.note: add a reason with parce que

items.hr: Pendant mon temps libre, je fais du sport et je lis beaucoup.
items.en: In my free time, I do sport and I read a lot.

items.hr: Plus tard, j'aimerais devenir professeure de langues.
items.en: Later, I would like to become a language teacher.
items.note: talk about plans with the conditionnel

items.hr: Ce qui me passionne, c'est de rencontrer de nouvelles cultures.
items.en: What excites me is meeting new cultures.

activities.title: Bien se présenter
activities.intro: Practise expanding and steering your answers.

questions.prompt: Dans l'entretien, il ne faut jamais répondre :
questions.explanation: One-word answers waste the chance to show your French; always expand.

questions.prompt: Pour développer une réponse, on peut ajouter :
questions.explanation: A reason and an example turn a short reply into a developed one.

questions.prompt: On justifie une réponse avec la conjonction « ___ ». (cause)
questions.explanation: Parce que introduces the reason for an answer.

questions.prompt: Rappel de la mise en relief : « Ce ___ me passionne, c'est la musique. »
questions.explanation: Ce qui opens a subject cleft, resumed by c'est.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I am learning French to travel.'
questions.explanation: Pour plus the infinitive voyager gives the purpose of learning.

activities.title: Vous pouvez vous présenter ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Answer an examiner's opening questions with developed replies.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, pouvez-vous vous présenter ?
lines.en: Hello, can you introduce yourself?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr, je m'appelle Léa et j'habite à Nantes.
lines.en: Of course, my name is Léa and I live in Nantes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourquoi apprenez-vous le français ?
lines.en: Why are you learning French?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parce que je voudrais étudier en France l'an prochain.
lines.en: Because I would like to study in France next year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que faites-vous pendant votre temps libre ?
lines.en: What do you do in your free time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'aime la photo et, ce qui me plaît le plus, c'est voyager.
lines.en: I like photography and what I enjoy most is travelling.

days.title: Oral : exprimer un point de vue, construire l'exposé
days.objective: Build a short structured talk from a topic prompt: state a position, give ordered arguments, and conclude.
days.paretoFocus: When you draw a topic and must speak, a quick clear structure spoken out loud beats a rich idea delivered as a muddle.
drills: Take a topic and choose a clear position within thirty seconds.
Give your talk a spoken structure: position, two arguments, conclusion.
Signpost each step aloud with premièrement, deuxièmement and pour finir.

items: What should you decide first when you draw a topic?
Which words help you signpost your arguments aloud?
How do you announce the conclusion of a spoken talk?

activities.title: Structurer à l'oral
activities.intro: For the monologue you draw a topic and speak for a few minutes. Choose one clear position quickly, structure it aloud like a mini essay, and signpost each step so the listener can follow. A weak position defended clearly beats a strong one delivered in a mess.

items.hr: Le sujet que j'ai tiré porte sur les réseaux sociaux.
items.en: The topic I drew is about social media.

items.hr: Je vais défendre l'idée qu'ils rapprochent les gens.
items.en: I'm going to defend the idea that they bring people closer.

items.hr: Premièrement, ils permettent de garder le contact à distance.
items.en: Firstly, they make it possible to keep in touch from afar.

items.hr: Deuxièmement, ils aident à s'informer rapidement.
items.en: Secondly, they help to get information quickly.

items.hr: Pour finir, je dirais que le plus important, c'est de bien les utiliser.
items.en: To finish, I'd say the most important thing is to use them well.

activities.title: Un exposé clair
activities.intro: Practise structuring and signposting a spoken talk.

questions.prompt: Au début d'un exposé, il vaut mieux :
questions.explanation: Stating your position early lets the listener follow your talk.

questions.prompt: Quel mot organise les arguments à l'oral ?
questions.explanation: Premièrement signposts the first argument.

questions.prompt: Rappel des relatives : pour reformuler le sujet, on dit « le sujet ___ j'ai tiré ».
questions.explanation: Tiré takes a direct object, so the relative is que.

questions.prompt: On annonce la conclusion orale avec « pour ___ ».
questions.explanation: Pour finir signposts the spoken conclusion.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I am going to defend this idea.'
questions.explanation: Je vais défendre announces the position you will support.

activities.title: J'ai tiré un sujet
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Help a panicking friend structure a talk.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai tiré un sujet sur le sport à l'école, au secours !
lines.en: I drew a topic on sport at school, help!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Calme-toi, choisis vite une position claire.
lines.en: Calm down, quickly choose a clear position.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je pense que le sport est essentiel pour les enfants.
lines.en: I think sport is essential for children.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, donne deux arguments et un exemple pour chacun.
lines.en: Perfect, give two arguments and one example for each.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et comment je termine ?
lines.en: And how do I finish?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Par une phrase qui résume ta position, avec « pour finir ».
lines.en: With a sentence that sums up your position, using 'to finish'.

days.title: Oral : défendre son point de vue face aux questions
days.objective: Defend your view when questioned, concede a point politely, and use fillers to gain thinking time.
days.paretoFocus: The examiner will push back, and staying calm, conceding gracefully and buying a second to think is what keeps your spoken score up under pressure.
drills: Practise three polite fillers you can say while you think of an answer.
Concede one point to an opposing view before answering it.
Ask for a question to be repeated in one polite sentence.

items: What can you say to gain a moment before answering?
How do you concede a point while keeping your own view?
How do you politely ask for a question to be repeated?

activities.title: Rester calme et nuancer
activities.intro: After your talk the examiner challenges you. Do not panic: you can partly agree before answering, ask for a question to be repeated, and use a short filler to think. Defending a view calmly matters more than winning the argument.

items.hr: C'est une bonne question, je dirais que cela dépend des situations.
items.en: That's a good question, I'd say it depends on the situation.

items.hr: Vous avez raison sur ce point, mais je pense quand même que...
items.en: You're right on that point, but I still think that...

items.hr: Pourriez-vous répéter la question, s'il vous plaît ?
items.en: Could you repeat the question, please?
items.note: ask for a repeat without stress

items.hr: Ce que je voulais dire, c'est que le problème est plus large.
items.en: What I meant is that the problem is broader.

items.hr: Je comprends votre avis, cependant je ne suis pas tout à fait d'accord.
items.en: I understand your view, however I don't entirely agree.

activities.title: Face à l'examinateur
activities.intro: Practise fillers, concessions and polite disagreement.

questions.prompt: Pour gagner un peu de temps avant de répondre, on peut dire :
questions.explanation: A short polite filler buys a moment to organise your answer.

questions.prompt: Pour concéder un point tout en gardant son avis, on dit :
options: vous avez raison, mais je pense que
questions.answer: vous avez raison, mais je pense que
questions.explanation: You concede with vous avez raison, then nuance with mais je pense que.

questions.prompt: Pour faire répéter poliment : « ___ -vous répéter, s'il vous plaît ? » (pouvoir, conditionnel, vous)
questions.explanation: The conditional Pourriez-vous makes the request polite.

questions.prompt: Rappel de la mise en relief : pour se reprendre, « Ce ___ je voulais dire, c'est... »
questions.explanation: Ce que opens an object cleft used to reformulate.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I understand, but I do not agree.'
questions.explanation: You acknowledge with je comprends, then disagree politely.

activities.title: On vous met au défi
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Defend a view calmly as the examiner pushes back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous dites que la ville est meilleure que la campagne, vraiment ?
lines.en: You say the city is better than the countryside, really?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est une bonne question, cela dépend des priorités de chacun.
lines.en: That's a good question, it depends on everyone's priorities.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mais la ville est plus polluée, non ?
lines.en: But the city is more polluted, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vous avez raison sur ce point, mais elle offre plus de travail.
lines.en: You're right on that point, but it offers more work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourtant, la vie y est plus stressante.
lines.en: Yet, life there is more stressful.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je comprends votre avis, cependant je préfère les services urbains.
lines.en: I understand your view, however I prefer urban services.

days.title: Examen blanc : gérer les quatre épreuves
days.objective: Manage a full mock exam by budgeting time across the four skills and applying the right strategy to each.
days.paretoFocus: Putting the four skills together under time pressure is its own skill, and a rehearsed plan for pacing and re-reading is what carries you through the real thing.
drills: Rehearse the order you will tackle the four parts and how long each lasts.
Start every writing task with a quick plan before drafting.
Keep a few minutes at the end of each written part to re-read.

items: What is the first thing to do before answering any part?
How should you start the written part?
Why keep a few minutes at the end of each section?

activities.title: Gérer tout l'examen
activities.intro: A full mock puts the four skills together. The goal is time management and calm: know how long each part lasts, start writing tasks with a quick plan, and keep a few minutes at the end of each written part to re-read. No single question is worth losing a whole section.

items.hr: Je commence par lire toutes les consignes avant de répondre.
items.en: I start by reading all the instructions before answering.

items.hr: Pour l'écoute, je note les mots-clés à chaque enregistrement.
items.en: For listening, I note the key words at each recording.

items.hr: Pour la lecture, je repère l'information sans tout lire.
items.en: For reading, I locate the information without reading everything.

items.hr: Pour l'écrit, je fais un plan rapide puis je rédige.
items.en: For writing, I make a quick plan then I write.

items.hr: Je garde cinq minutes à la fin pour tout relire.
items.en: I keep five minutes at the end to re-read everything.

activities.title: Une stratégie d'ensemble
activities.intro: Confirm your plan for managing the whole exam.

questions.prompt: Avant de répondre à une épreuve, la première chose à faire est :
options: dessiner un plan de la salle
questions.explanation: Reading the instructions first prevents avoidable mistakes.

questions.prompt: Pour la partie écrite, il vaut mieux commencer par :
questions.explanation: A quick plan gives the writing a clear structure.

questions.prompt: À la fin de chaque partie, il faut garder du temps pour la ___.
questions.explanation: A final re-read catches errors before you hand in.

questions.prompt: Rappel de la stratégie d'écoute : pour l'écoute, on note surtout les ___-clés.
questions.explanation: Noting key words is the core listening strategy.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I read all the instructions first.'
questions.explanation: D'abord marks reading the instructions as the first step.

activities.title: Après l'examen blanc
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Compare how you each managed the mock exam.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, comment s'est passé ton examen blanc ?
lines.en: So, how did your mock exam go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Plutôt bien, j'ai réussi à gérer mon temps.
lines.en: Pretty well, I managed my time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Moi, j'ai passé trop de temps sur la lecture.
lines.en: Me, I spent too much time on the reading.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il faut repérer l'info sans tout lire, ça va plus vite.
lines.en: You have to locate the info without reading everything, it's faster.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour l'écrit, tu fais comment ?
lines.en: And for the writing, how do you do it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un plan rapide, puis je rédige, et je relis à la fin.
lines.en: A quick plan, then I write, and I re-read at the end.

days.title: La mode et l'apparence
days.objective: Describe how someone looks and dresses, and defend a taste in clothes with a reason.
days.paretoFocus: Talking about appearance and style comes up in every casual conversation, and it lets you state a preference and back it up.
drills: Describe an outfit you like using une tenue, and say why in one sentence.
Write two opinions about fashion, one for and one against following trends.
Pick three of today's words (apparence, chic, elegant) and use each in a full sentence.

items: Which word means a whole outfit rather than a single garment?
How would you say that someone is dressed with taste?
Give the feminine form of the adjective elegant.

activities.title: Soigner son apparence
activities.intro: These sentences let you describe looks and style and, more importantly, give an opinion with a reason. Notice that each one states a preference and justifies it, which is exactly what B1 asks of you.

items.hr: Je trouve qu'une tenue simple est plus élégante qu'un habit trop chargé, parce que la sobriété attire le regard.
items.en: I think a simple outfit is more elegant than an over-decorated garment, because plainness draws the eye.
items.note: une tenue = a whole outfit; un habit = a garment

items.hr: Elle a beaucoup de goût : elle choisit toujours des vêtements chic sans dépenser une fortune.
items.en: She has a lot of taste: she always chooses chic clothes without spending a fortune.

items.hr: Ce blouson te va bien, mais je préfère quand tu portes des couleurs plus discrètes.
items.en: That jacket suits you, but I prefer it when you wear more understated colours.

items.hr: Franchement, courir après la mode coûte cher et je pense que ça ne rend personne plus heureux.
items.en: Honestly, chasing fashion is expensive and I think it makes nobody happier.

items.hr: Avec ses cheveux blonds et son style soigné, on la remarque tout de suite dans une pièce.
items.en: With her blond hair and her polished style, you notice her at once in a room.

activities.title: Choisir et juger un style
activities.intro: Practise vocabulary of appearance and stating a justified preference.

questions.prompt: Je préfère une ___ simple : à mon avis, la sobriété est plus élégante.
questions.explanation: Une tenue is a whole outfit; the sentence is about clothing.

questions.prompt: Recall of the subjunctive: Il faut que tu ___ une veste plus habillée pour cette soirée.
questions.explanation: Il faut que triggers the subjunctive, so mettre becomes que tu mettes.

questions.prompt: Elle s'habille avec beaucoup de goût, ses vêtements sont toujours très ___ et discrets.
questions.explanation: Élégant agrees with the plural masculine vetements: elegants.

questions.prompt: Ce ___ en cuir est un peu court, mais il te va vraiment bien.
questions.explanation: Un blouson is a short jacket, the garment described here.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I think a plain outfit is more elegant.'
questions.explanation: Je trouve que introduces the opinion, and elegante agrees with tenue.

activities.title: Deux styles
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A mate says following fashion is worth the trouble; you are not so sure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as vu sa tenue ? Je la trouve super chic, j'aimerais m'habiller comme ça.
lines.en: Did you see her outfit? I find it really chic, I would like to dress like that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Elle est élégante, c'est vrai, mais suivre la mode coûte une fortune et ça ne dure jamais.
lines.en: She is elegant, that is true, but following fashion costs a fortune and it never lasts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Peut-être, mais soigner son apparence donne confiance en soi, non ?
lines.en: Maybe, but taking care of your appearance gives self-confidence, doesn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne suis pas contre être soigné, au contraire. Je dis juste qu'un style simple suffit.
lines.en: I am not against being well-groomed, quite the opposite. I am just saying that a simple style is enough.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Donc pour toi, ce blouson tout neuf, c'est de l'argent gaspillé ?
lines.en: So for you, that brand-new jacket is wasted money?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas forcément. S'il te plaît et que tu le gardes longtemps, alors ça vaut le coup.
lines.en: Not necessarily. If you like it and keep it a long time, then it is worth it.

days.title: Les sentiments en nuance
days.objective: Name shades of feeling such as shame, pride and hope, and explain what caused them.
days.paretoFocus: Precise emotion words let you say not just that you feel bad or good, but exactly which feeling and why, which every real conversation needs.
drills: Write three sentences, each naming a feeling (la honte, la fierté, l'espoir) and its cause.
Describe a moment of disappointment using j'ai été déçu parce que.
Turn one negative feeling into a hope, using mais j'espère que.

items: Which noun names the feeling of being proud?
How do you say you were disappointed by something?
Give a verb that expresses reassuring someone.

activities.title: Mettre un mot sur ce qu'on ressent
activities.intro: Feelings become useful when you attach a cause to them. Each sentence below names an emotion and says what produced it, and several take a clear stance about it.

items.hr: J'éprouve une certaine fierté quand mon travail est reconnu, et je pense que c'est un sentiment sain.
items.en: I feel a certain pride when my work is recognised, and I think it is a healthy feeling.
items.note: éprouver un sentiment = to feel an emotion

items.hr: Il a ressenti de la honte après sa réaction, parce qu'il savait qu'il avait exagéré.
items.en: He felt shame after his reaction, because he knew he had overreacted.

items.hr: Malgré le chagrin du départ, elle garde l'espoir de les revoir bientôt.
items.en: Despite the sorrow of leaving, she keeps the hope of seeing them again soon.

items.hr: J'ai été déçu par le film, mais je préfère le dire calmement plutôt que de me plaindre.
items.en: I was disappointed by the film, but I prefer to say it calmly rather than to complain.

items.hr: Quand un ami a peur, le mieux est de le rassurer sans minimiser sa crainte.
items.en: When a friend is afraid, the best thing is to reassure them without playing down their fear.

activities.title: Nommer l'émotion et sa cause
activities.intro: Practise emotion nouns and verbs and linking each to a cause.

questions.prompt: Il a ressenti de la ___ après avoir menti, car il n'était pas fier de lui.
questions.explanation: La honte is shame, the feeling that fits lying and not being proud.

questions.prompt: Reprise du subjonctif : Je crains qu'il ne ___ pas à temps.
questions.explanation: Craindre que triggers the subjunctive: qu'il vienne.

questions.prompt: Le spectacle m'a déçu : j'ai vraiment été ___ par la fin, trop rapide.
questions.explanation: Déçu is the past participle used as an adjective, agreeing with a masculine speaker.

questions.prompt: Elle a perdu son emploi, pourtant elle garde l'___ de retrouver mieux ailleurs.
questions.explanation: L'espoir is hope, the noun that contrasts with the loss.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I feel a certain pride when my work is recognised.'
questions.explanation: Éprouver un sentiment plus quand introduces the trigger of the feeling.

activities.title: Le concours raté
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your friend failed an exam and you weigh how to react.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai raté mon concours et j'ai tellement honte que je n'ose plus en parler.
lines.en: I failed my competitive exam and I am so ashamed that I no longer dare talk about it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je comprends le chagrin, mais la honte n'est pas justifiée : tu as travaillé dur.
lines.en: I understand the sorrow, but the shame is not justified: you worked hard.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Facile à dire. Toi, tu as réussi du premier coup.
lines.en: Easy to say. You, you passed on the first try.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est vrai, et je ne veux pas minimiser ta déception. Mais un échec n'efface pas ta valeur.
lines.en: That is true, and I do not want to play down your disappointment. But one failure does not erase your worth.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu penses vraiment que je devrais retenter ?
lines.en: Do you really think I should try again?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, garde l'espoir. À mon avis, tu as toutes tes chances la prochaine fois.
lines.en: Yes, keep hope. In my opinion, you have every chance next time.

days.title: Les relations amoureuses et l'amitié
days.objective: Talk about friendship and romantic relationships and give a view on what makes them last.
days.paretoFocus: Relationships are one of the most common topics of real talk, and the vocabulary lets you narrate and take a position at the same time.
drills: Describe a friendship using un copain or une amie and one quality you value.
Write two sentences about what makes a couple last, one you agree with, one you doubt.
Use se marier and s'entendre bien in the same short story.

items: Which everyday word means a male friend or mate?
How do you say two people get along well?
Give a verb that means to charm or seduce someone.

activities.title: Ce qui unit les gens
activities.intro: Relationship talk is built on a few verbs and nouns. The sentences below narrate and, in most cases, defend an idea about what holds people together.

items.hr: Un vrai copain, c'est quelqu'un sur qui tu peux compter, pas seulement quelqu'un avec qui tu sors.
items.en: A real mate is someone you can count on, not just someone you go out with.

items.hr: Ils se sont mariés très jeunes, et pourtant ils s'entendent encore parfaitement.
items.en: They married very young, and yet they still get along perfectly.

items.hr: Je pense que l'amitié dure quand chacun accepte les défauts de l'autre sans vouloir le changer.
items.en: I think friendship lasts when each one accepts the other's flaws without wanting to change them.

items.hr: Il a essayé de la séduire avec de beaux discours, mais elle préfère les gens sincères.
items.en: He tried to charm her with fine speeches, but she prefers sincere people.

items.hr: Quand on aime vraiment quelqu'un, on l'embrasse le matin sans raison particulière.
items.en: When you really love someone, you kiss them in the morning for no particular reason.

activities.title: Amitié et amour
activities.intro: Practise relationship vocabulary and expressing a reasoned opinion.

questions.prompt: Un vrai ___, c'est quelqu'un sur qui on peut compter dans les moments difficiles.
questions.explanation: Un copain is a friend or mate, the person you can rely on.

questions.prompt: Rappel des relatifs : C'est la personne ___ je te parlais hier soir.
questions.explanation: Parler de quelqu'un takes de, so the relative is dont.

questions.prompt: Ils ne sont pas seulement amoureux : surtout, ils s'___ très bien au quotidien.
questions.explanation: S'entendre bien means to get along; with ils it is s'entendent.

questions.prompt: Il a tout fait pour la ___, mais elle reste indifférente à ses compliments.
questions.explanation: Séduire is the infinitive after pour, meaning to charm or win over.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'A real mate is someone you can count on.'
questions.explanation: Compter sur quelqu'un keeps sur qui before the verb group.

activities.title: Est-ce que ça dure ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend thinks marrying young is wise; you push back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mes voisins se sont mariés à vingt ans. Je trouve ça courageux.
lines.en: My neighbours got married at twenty. I find that brave.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Courageux, oui, mais risqué : à vingt ans, on ne se connaît pas encore vraiment.
lines.en: Brave, yes, but risky: at twenty, you do not really know yourself yet.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pourtant ils s'entendent très bien depuis dix ans.
lines.en: Yet they have got along very well for ten years.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est vrai, et j'admets que ça marche pour eux. Mais ce n'est pas une règle générale.
lines.en: That is true, and I admit that it works for them. But it is not a general rule.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors selon toi, il vaut mieux attendre ?
lines.en: So according to you, it is better to wait?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À mon avis, oui : l'amour dure mieux quand chacun sait déjà qui il est.
lines.en: In my opinion, yes: love lasts better when each person already knows who they are.

days.title: La santé mentale et le stress
days.objective: Talk about stress and mental wellbeing and argue for a way of coping.
days.paretoFocus: Stress is a universal topic, and this vocabulary lets you describe how you feel and defend a strategy for handling it.
drills: Describe a stressful week using la pression and one physical effect.
Write two coping ideas and say which one convinces you and why.
Reassure a stressed friend in two sentences using tu devrais and ça va aller.

items: Which noun names the pressure you feel before a deadline?
How do you say you feel exhausted?
Give a verb that means to calm down.

activities.title: Quand la pression monte
activities.intro: This vocabulary describes mental strain and ways out of it. Notice how each sentence not only names a feeling but usually recommends or judges a response.

items.hr: Sous la pression du travail, je dors mal et je fais parfois des cauchemars.
items.en: Under work pressure, I sleep badly and I sometimes have nightmares.
items.note: la pression = pressure; un cauchemar = a nightmare

items.hr: Je pense qu'il vaut mieux parler de son stress que de le garder pour soi.
items.en: I think it is better to talk about your stress than to keep it to yourself.

items.hr: Quand je suis complètement fatigué, une simple marche m'aide à me calmer.
items.en: When I am completely exhausted, a simple walk helps me to calm down.

items.hr: Elle vit dans une tension permanente et finit par tomber malade à force de trop en faire.
items.en: She lives under constant tension and ends up getting sick from doing too much.
items.note: à force de = through repeatedly doing

items.hr: Le meilleur conseil, à mon avis, c'est de ralentir avant que le corps ne dise stop.
items.en: The best advice, in my opinion, is to slow down before the body says stop.

activities.title: Gérer la pression
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of stress and recommending a response.

questions.prompt: Avant les examens, je dors mal et je fais souvent des ___.
questions.explanation: Un cauchemar is a nightmare, which fits sleeping badly before exams.

questions.prompt: Gérondif déjà vu : Je me détends ___ de la musique douce.
questions.explanation: The gérondif en écoutant expresses the means of relaxing.

questions.prompt: Le soir, une tisane et un livre m'aident à me ___ après une journée tendue.
questions.explanation: Se calmer means to calm down; after aident à me it is the infinitive calmer.

questions.prompt: Il travaille trop et vit sous une ___ constante qui l'empêche de se reposer.
questions.explanation: La pression is the pressure that stops him from resting.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'It is better to talk about your stress than to keep it.'
questions.explanation: Il vaut mieux X que de Y compares two courses of action.

activities.title: Trop, c'est trop
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague is overworked and you weigh two ways to cope.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je suis complètement fatigué. Je dors mal et la pression ne baisse jamais.
lines.en: I am completely exhausted. I sleep badly and the pressure never drops.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu devrais en parler à ton chef. Garder tout pour soi, ça n'aide personne.
lines.en: You should talk to your boss about it. Keeping everything to yourself helps no one.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai peur qu'il pense que je ne suis pas capable.
lines.en: I am afraid he might think I am not capable.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je comprends la crainte, mais continuer comme ça est pire : tu vas tomber malade.
lines.en: I understand the fear, but going on like this is worse: you are going to get sick.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Peut-être que je devrais juste prendre des vacances à la place.
lines.en: Maybe I should just take a holiday instead.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Les vacances aident sur le moment, mais elles ne changeront pas la charge de travail.
lines.en: Holidays help in the moment, but they will not change the workload.

days.title: Le sommeil et le rythme de vie
days.objective: Describe your daily rhythm and sleep, and argue about early versus late routines.
days.paretoFocus: Everyone talks about being tired or short of sleep, and these time-and-rhythm words let you narrate your day and defend your habits.
drills: Describe your usual evening using le lendemain and one time word.
Write two sentences comparing going to bed early and staying up late.
Say what you did last night using je me suis endormi and one detail.

items: Which word means the day after?
How do you say midnight and midday?
Give a verb meaning to fall asleep.

activities.title: Bien dormir, mieux vivre
activities.intro: These sentences describe sleep and the rhythm of a day, and most of them take a side on how to organise it. The time words here are extremely common.

items.hr: Je me couche avant minuit, sinon le lendemain je suis bon à rien.
items.en: I go to bed before midnight, otherwise the next day I am good for nothing.

items.hr: Elle s'endort tard et se réveille à midi : son rythme ne convient pas à un travail de bureau.
items.en: She falls asleep late and wakes up at noon: her rhythm does not suit an office job.
items.note: s'endormir = to fall asleep; midi = noon

items.hr: À mon avis, une courte sieste l'après-midi est plus utile qu'un long grasse matinée.
items.en: In my opinion, a short nap in the afternoon is more useful than a long lie-in.
items.note: la matinée = the morning as a stretch of time

items.hr: La veille d'un examen, je préfère dormir plutôt que de réviser toute la nuit.
items.en: The night before an exam, I prefer to sleep rather than to revise all night.

items.hr: Mon voisin ronfle si fort que je l'entends à travers le mur.
items.en: My neighbour snores so loudly that I hear him through the wall.

activities.title: Rythmes et horaires
activities.intro: Practise time words and defending a routine.

questions.prompt: Je me couche tôt, sinon le ___ je n'arrive pas à me concentrer.
questions.explanation: Le lendemain is the next day, which fits the consequence of a late night.

questions.prompt: Si + imparfait, déjà vu : Si je ___ plus tôt, je serais moins fatigué.
questions.explanation: The hypothesis si + imparfait pairs with a conditional main clause.

questions.prompt: Hier, j'étais si fatigué que je me suis ___ devant la télévision.
questions.explanation: S'endormir in the passé composé with a masculine subject gives je me suis endormi.

questions.prompt: La ___ de la réunion, couche-toi tôt pour être en forme.
questions.explanation: La veille is the day before an event.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I go to bed before midnight, otherwise I am tired.'
questions.explanation: Sinon links the habit to its consequence; avant minuit sets the limit.

activities.title: Couche-tard contre lève-tôt
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You defend going to bed early against a friend who loves the night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Moi, je suis plus créatif la nuit. Je m'endors vers deux heures du matin.
lines.en: Me, I am more creative at night. I fall asleep around two in the morning.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je comprends, mais le lendemain tu es épuisé. Le corps aime la régularité.
lines.en: I understand, but the next day you are exhausted. The body likes regularity.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Toi tu te couches avant minuit, mais tu rates les meilleures idées.
lines.en: You go to bed before midnight, but you miss the best ideas.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Possible, sauf que mes idées du matin sont plus claires, parce que j'ai bien dormi.
lines.en: Possibly, except that my morning ideas are clearer, because I slept well.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Donc pour toi, une sieste ne remplace pas une vraie nuit ?
lines.en: So for you, a nap does not replace a real night's sleep?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Exactement. Une sieste dépanne, mais rien ne remplace un sommeil régulier.
lines.en: Exactly. A nap helps out, but nothing replaces regular sleep.

days.title: Les générations et la transmission
days.objective: Talk about family generations and what gets passed down, and give a view on tradition.
days.paretoFocus: Family and generations come up constantly, and this vocabulary lets you narrate a family history and argue about what is worth keeping.
drills: Describe your grandparents using mes grands-parents and one thing they taught you.
Write two sentences on whether traditions should be kept, one for, one against.
Tell a short family story using grandir and vieillir.

items: Which nouns name the grandfather and grandmother?
How do you say to grow up and to grow old?
Give the word for the eldest child.

activities.title: Ce qui se transmet
activities.intro: These sentences talk about family across generations and take positions about tradition. The kinship words here are very frequent and were missing from your deck.

items.hr: Mon grand-père m'a transmis le goût du travail bien fait, et j'en suis reconnaissant.
items.en: My grandfather passed on to me the taste for a job well done, and I am grateful for it.
items.note: transmettre = to pass down; en replaces de cela

items.hr: Ma grand-mère a grandi à la campagne et raconte souvent son enfance.
items.en: My grandmother grew up in the countryside and often tells of her childhood.
items.note: grandir = to grow up; l'enfance = childhood

items.hr: Je pense qu'une tradition ne vaut la peine d'être gardée que si elle a encore un sens.
items.en: I think a tradition is only worth keeping if it still has meaning.

items.hr: En vieillissant, mon oncle est devenu plus patient avec ses neveux.
items.en: As he grows older, my uncle has become more patient with his nephews.
items.note: vieillir = to grow old; le neveu = nephew

items.hr: L'aînée de la famille garde les photos et raconte l'histoire aux plus jeunes.
items.en: The eldest of the family keeps the photos and tells the story to the younger ones.

activities.title: Familles et héritages
activities.intro: Practise kinship vocabulary and opinions about tradition.

questions.prompt: Mon ___ a quatre-vingt-dix ans et raconte encore sa jeunesse avec précision.
questions.explanation: Grand-père is the grandfather, fitting the memory of youth.

questions.prompt: Passé composé déjà vu : Les photos que ma tante m'a ___ sont précieuses.
questions.explanation: The preposed COD que (les photos, feminine plural) forces the agreement données.

questions.prompt: Ma grand-mère a ___ dans un petit village avant de partir en ville.
questions.explanation: Grandir in the passé composé with avoir gives a grandi.

questions.prompt: En ___, on devient parfois plus tolérant qu'on ne l'était jeune.
questions.explanation: The gérondif of vieillir is en vieillissant, meaning as one grows old.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'My grandfather passed on to me the taste for work.'
questions.explanation: Transmettre quelque chose à quelqu'un: here m'a transmis le goût.

activities.title: Garder ou tourner la page
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You and a cousin weigh whether to keep an old family tradition.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Notre grand-mère tenait à ce grand repas chaque année. On devrait le garder.
lines.en: Our grandmother cared about that big meal every year. We should keep it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'idée me plaît, mais franchement, personne n'a plus le temps de tout préparer.
lines.en: The idea appeals to me, but honestly, nobody has the time to prepare it all anymore.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est justement ça, la transmission : on fait un effort pour les anciens.
lines.en: That is exactly what transmission is: you make an effort for the elders.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis d'accord sur le principe. Mais une tradition n'a de sens que si elle nous réunit vraiment.
lines.en: I agree in principle. But a tradition only makes sense if it truly brings us together.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors on la garde, mais en plus simple ?
lines.en: So we keep it, but simpler?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Voilà. On garde l'esprit, on allège le reste. Comme ça, les jeunes viendront aussi.
lines.en: That's it. We keep the spirit, we lighten the rest. That way the young ones will come too.

days.title: La cuisine et les recettes
days.objective: Give and follow a recipe using the imperative, and defend a taste in food.
days.paretoFocus: Cooking instructions are a natural home for the imperative, and food is one of the richest everyday topics for stating preferences.
drills: Write a three-step recipe using imperatives (coupe, ajoute, fais cuire).
Describe a dish you love using le goût and one reason.
Give a friend two cooking tips using the imperative.

items: Which verb means to cook something by heat?
How do you tell someone to cut the vegetables?
Give the noun for taste or flavour.

activities.title: À vos fourneaux
activities.intro: A recipe is mostly imperatives plus food words. The sentences below give instructions and, at the end, defend a choice of taste, which keeps the opinion muscle working.

items.hr: Coupe les légumes en petits morceaux, puis fais-les cuire à feu doux.
items.en: Cut the vegetables into small pieces, then cook them over low heat.
items.note: cuire = to cook; imperative coupe, fais

items.hr: Ajoute un peu de crème à la fin pour adoucir le goût de la sauce.
items.en: Add a little cream at the end to soften the taste of the sauce.
items.note: la crème = cream; le goût = taste

items.hr: Ne fais pas trop griller la viande, sinon elle devient dure.
items.en: Do not grill the meat too much, otherwise it becomes tough.

items.hr: À mon avis, un plat simple mais bien assaisonné vaut mieux qu'une recette compliquée.
items.en: In my opinion, a simple but well-seasoned dish is better than a complicated recipe.

items.hr: Goûte avant de servir : il vaut mieux corriger le sel maintenant qu'à table.
items.en: Taste before serving: it is better to fix the salt now than at the table.

activities.title: Suivre la recette
activities.intro: Practise the imperative and food vocabulary.

questions.prompt: ___ les oignons finement avant de les faire revenir dans l'huile.
questions.explanation: The tu imperative of couper drops the final s: Coupe.

questions.prompt: Pronom déjà vu : Tu as goûté la sauce ? Oui, je ___ ai ajouté du sel.
questions.explanation: Ajouter à la sauce takes à, so the place is replaced by y.

questions.prompt: Fais ___ la viande quelques minutes de chaque côté, sans la brûler.
questions.explanation: Faire cuire means to cook; cuire is the infinitive after fais.

questions.prompt: Ajoute un peu de crème pour adoucir le ___ un peu acide de la sauce.
questions.explanation: Le goût is the taste or flavour being softened.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Cut the vegetables, then cook them over low heat.'
questions.explanation: Two imperatives joined by puis; fais-les cuire keeps the pronoun attached.

activities.title: Une question de goût
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend swears by fancy recipes; you defend simple cooking.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'adore les recettes compliquées, avec dix ingrédients et une belle présentation.
lines.en: I love complicated recipes, with ten ingredients and a nice presentation.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Chacun ses goûts, mais pour moi, un plat trop compliqué cache souvent le vrai goût.
